Title: Claims Risk Analyst
Description: Introduction Through our client-facing brands Metropolitan and Momentum, with Multiply (wellness and rewards programme), and our other specialist brands, including Guardrisk and Eris Property Group, the group enables business and people from all walks of life to achieve their financial goals and life aspirations. We help people grow their savings, protect what matters to them and invest for the future. We help companies and organisations care for and reward their employees and members. Through our own network of advisers or via independent brokers and utilising new platforms Momentum Metropolitan provides practical financial solutions for people, communities, and businesses. Disclaimer As an applicant, please verify the legitimacy of this job advert on our company career page. Role Purpose To conduct desktop based forensic investigations into potential instances of fraud, waste and or abuse across Momentum Health admisitered schemes and to collect documentory evidence, engage with all relevant parties i.e., service providers, members etc. drafting letters and investigation reports, perform data analytics and assit in the recovery of irregular paid claims. Requirements Qualification Valid Matric certificate. A relevant tertiary qualification such as, Clinical Healthcare related, Law, including Compliance, Business Operations e.g., auditing, accounting & operations. Knowledge Medical Scheme knowledge would be advantages. Years of experience Three years of forensic experience. Duties & Responsibilities Internal Process Conducting desktop forensic investigation in order to identify potential instances of fraud, waste and or abuse across MH administered schemes. Reporting on investigations conducted by submitting an investigation report to management for review and submission to the relevant scheme. Engage with relevant stakeholders (internal & external) to collect documentary evidence on instances of fraud, waste and or abuse identified. Draft letters providers members to raise concerns with regards to irregularities identified Perform data analytics to identify concerning trends on each investigation using the analytical tool available. Client Build and maintain relationships with internal and external stakeholders. Deliver on service level agreements applicable to clients, internal and external stakeholders in order to ensure that client expectations are managed. Make recommendations to improve claims processes controls where deficiencies in these areas are highlighted through investigations. Participate and contribute to a culture which builds rewarding relationships, facilitates feedback and provides exceptional client service. Continuously monitor turnaround times and quality standards and resolve issues speedily to enhance client service delivery. Drive client service delivery goal achievement in line with predefined standards in order to ensure that clients receive appropriate advice and after sales service. Manage the assigned investigation to ensure that fraud waste and abuse risks are mitigated through identification and investigation of potential fraud waste and or abuse. People Build strong relationships with peers and managers to ensure successful investigation outcomes. Continuously develop own expertise in terms of industry and subject matter development and application thereof in an area of specialisation. Contribute to continuous innovation through the development, sharing and or implementation of new ideas and involvement of colleagues and staff. Participate and contribute to a culture of work centric thinking, productivity, service delivery and quality management. Take ownership for driving own career development. Finance Assist in the recovery of irregular paid claims through engagement with providers and obtaining provider agreement on the repayment of irregular claims. Identify solutions to enhance cost effectiveness and increase operational efficiency. Competencies Analytical thinking Healthcare industry knowledge (advantageous) Time management Attention to detail Problem-solving Communication Confidentiality Adherence to regulations Maturity: Display a high level of emotional maturity, professionalism, and soundjudgment in handling sensitive cases and maintaining confi dentiality as well asinterpersonal offi ce relationships. Hardworking: Demonstrate a strong work ethic, dedication, and a commitment tomeeting deadlines and delivering quality results. Professionalism: Uphold the highest standards of professionalism, ethics, andintegrity, with a focus on the job's core responsibilities rather than engaging indisruptive, insubordinate, or disrespectful practices.

Title: Senior Application Support Analyst (SQL-focused)
Description: MRI Software is seeking a Senior Application Support Analyst (SQL-focused) to join our team in Cape Town. This role is ideal for someone with strong hands-on experience writing SQL and PL SQL, who can independently analyse complex issues, develop queries, and deliver data-driven solutions to clients. As a senior member of the team, you will take ownership of complex, database-driven support cases, acting as a key escalation point. You will be responsible for writing, reviewing, and optimising SQL statements, diagnosing issues at source, and ensuring long-term, scalable resolutions. This role is highly technical, with a strong focus on query development, database analysis, and performance optimisation, alongside client engagement. The role is adhering to UK hours and public holidays. We understand the need to provide a flexible working environment partnered with team collaboration and socialisation . Therefore, we operate a hybrid working model with 3 days of working from home per week. This role is based in the Cape Town office. Key Responsibilities Write, troubleshoot, and optimise SQL queries, scripts, and PL SQL code to resolve client issues Analyse complex data-related problems using custom-built SQL statements and queries Act as a technical escalation point for advanced database and application issues Perform detailed root cause analysis, identifying issues within database structures, queries, or data integrity Review and improve existing queries to enhance performance, accuracy, and efficiency Work directly with clients to gather requirements and translate them into SQL-based solutions Ensure delivery against SLAs and KPIs, maintaining high levels of client satisfaction Contribute to documentation, including SQL scripts, troubleshooting steps, and best practices Mentor junior analysts, particularly in SQL development and debugging approaches Participate in UAT, validating data fixes and database-related changes Required Experience & Skills 4–6+ years’ experience in application or technical support within enterprise software Strong, practical experience writing complex SQL queries from scratch Hands-on experience with Oracle PL SQL, including developing stored procedures, triggers, and packages Proven ability to build, modify, and optimise SQL statements to solve real business problems Experience with query performance tuning (indexes, execution plans, optimisation techniques) Strong understanding of relational database design and data structures Ability to work with large datasets and extract meaningful insights via SQL Strong problem-solving skills with a logical, structured approach Ability to clearly communicate technical solutions to non-technical stakeholders Fluent in English We’re obsessed with making this the best job you’ve ever had! We want our teams to love working here, so we’ve created some incredible perks for you to enjoy: We want our staff to love working here, and so we’ve created a few unique perks such as office breakfasts, quarterly lunches and virtual social events. Additionally, we value your input in your employee experience and have employee-led groups such as our DEI committee, employee resource groups such as Women and Allies, and our Pride Event Group Have confidence in your health with our offered Medical Aid Scheme. Invest in our competitive Personal Pension plan and help set you up for your future. Big on family? So are we! Here at MRI Software we recognise that your family is important, and being able to spend quality time with your family as it grows is a wonderful experience. Therefore, the MRI Software Parental Leave benefit is designed to give you the opportunity to spend time with your new arrival(s). Enjoy a fantastic work-life balance with 25 days of annual leave plus Public Holidays, in addition to a bank of 16 hours of "Flex Time Off" to be used whenever and however you choose! Further your professional development with our Tuition Reimbursement Schemes Enjoy the flexibility of working from anywhere in the world for two weeks out of the year! About Us From the day we opened our doors, MRI Software has built flexible, game-changing real estate software that powers thriving communities and helps make the world a better place to live, work and play. Fulfilling that mission is only possible because of one thing: exceptional people. People like you! Our people-first approach to PropTech is defining a new industry standard for client experiences that, quite frankly, can’t be duplicated. Experiences that deliver real value every day. And we know those experiences begin with our people. We believe MRI is more than just a workplace; it’s a connected community of people who truly feel they belong. Whether we’re investing in employee resource groups or providing tailored resources for each person to reach their full potential, we’re passionate about creating a work environment that makes you excited to show up every single day. At MRI, one of our core values is to strive to amaze. From the intelligent solutions we create to the culture we cultivate, that’s our goal every day. Because that’s what industry leaders do. Whether you’re joining as a new Pride member or rejoining us after a short time away, your talent is vital to us, our partners and our clients. Amazing growth requires amazing employees. Are you up to the challenge? We know confidence gap and imposter syndrome can get in the way of meeting remarkable candidates, so please don’t hesitate to apply. We’d love to hear from you! MRI is proud to be an inclusive employer. We welcome and celebrate diversity across all backgrounds, including ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, disability, age, military, veteran status and more. We believe that Belonging is a direct result of Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion. Those values are woven into the fabric of who we are and are foundational to our continued success. Come and see for yourself!

Title: Business Intelligence Analyst
Description: Bring 3+ years of proven experience in business intelligence, analytics, data analysis, Turn data into practical insight across marketing, product, and broader business operations Identify opportunities for AI and automation to improve workflows Work cross-functionally with Product, Engineering, Finance, Marketing, and other business units to solve real problems V&A Waterfront, Cape Town (hybrid) LekkeSlaap is South Africa’s leading accommodation booking app, known for its user-friendliness, exceptional customer experience, and extensive local accommodation options. Owned by Tripco , a travel technology company based in the V&A Waterfront, Cape Town, LekkeSlaap is committed to innovation, best-in-class customer service, and setting new standards in travel technology. As a Business Intelligence Analyst at Tripco, you’ll play a key role in helping the business use data more practically, proactively, and effectively. You’ll work closely with stakeholders across various business functions to turn data into clear insights, stronger decision-making, and real business value, with the opportunity to help shape and grow the data function over time. You’ll help the business not only understand what has happened, but also identify what should happen next. You’ll proactively seek out opportunities, inefficiencies, emerging trends, competitive insights, and areas of risk by combining internal and external data sources to support better business outcomes. This role is suited to someone who is not only strong technically, but also highly commercial and curious. You should enjoy investigating patterns, spotting opportunities, challenging assumptions, and turning complex information into clear recommendations that people can act on. You’ll also play an active role in identifying opportunities to use AI-driven tools and automation to improve internal workflows, analysis, and business performance as the industry continues to evolve. Our Environment You’ll work across a varied data and analytics environment, likely including: Business Intelligence & Reporting: BI tools, dashboards, recurring reporting, and ad hoc analysis Data & Analytics: SQL-based data sources, digital analytics, booking and commercial performance data Marketing & Growth: Google Analytics GA4, campaign and funnel performance, channel and conversion insight AI, Data & Automation: Active use of AI-driven tools, automation, and data-informed solutions to improve internal processes, analytical workflows, and business decision-making Cross-functional collaboration: Close partnership with Product, Engineering, Finance, Marketing, and Operational teams to translate business questions into clear analysis and action Responsibilities Business Insight & Analysis: Investigate trends, anomalies, and opportunities across the business and wider market, translating findings into practical recommendations that improve customer experience, commercial performance, operational efficiency, and strategic decision-making. Opportunity Discovery & Market Intelligence: Proactively identify opportunities for growth, optimisation, and innovation by analysing customer behaviour, market trends, competitor activity, operational performance, and emerging technologies. Surface insights that help the business prioritise investments and strategic initiatives. Reporting & Dashboarding: Design, maintain, and improve dashboards and recurring reporting that are clear, trusted, and genuinely useful to stakeholders. AI & Automation Enablement: Actively identify opportunities to introduce AI-assisted workflows, automation, and smarter analytical processes to improve efficiency and business impact. Data Integrity & Validation: Identify inconsistencies, gaps, and risks in data, reporting, tracking, modelling, and querying, helping strengthen governance, privacy, responsible AI usage, and trust in business-critical metrics. Stakeholder Engagement: Partner with stakeholders across the business to understand their questions, shape analysis, and communicate findings clearly. Problem Investigation: Proactively find the needle in the haystack by exploring what is changing in the business and around it, why it is changing, and what should happen next. Documentation: Maintain clear and practical documentation around key metrics, reporting logic, definitions, and analytical context to support consistency and better decision-making. Continuous Improvement: Contribute to better analytical practices, reporting habits, and the data roadmap, strengthening the use of data across the business. Requirements Behavioural competencies Collaboration: Thrives in a team environment, working effectively across multiple disciplines and building strong relationships with both technical and non-technical stakeholders. Commercial curiosity: Naturally asks smart questions, looks for underlying drivers, and cares about how data can create practical business value. Problem-solving mindset: Approaches challenges with a doer mentality, balancing analysis, judgment, and hands-on execution to deliver useful outcomes. Adaptability: Comfortable working in a fast-moving environment, handling ambiguity, and learning new tools, technologies, and ways of working as the business evolves. Urgency & ownership: Biased to action and manages workload effectively, takes initiative, and takes full ownership of analysis, outputs, and follow-through. Continuous learning: Proactively keeps skills and knowledge current, with strong interest in data, AI, automation, and evolving analytics practices. Attention to quality: Committed to producing accurate, thoughtful, and well-structured work that stakeholders can trust and act on. Functional experience & competencies 3+ years of proven experience in business intelligence, analytics, data analysis, or a similar role, ideally in a commercial, digital, e-commerce, travel, hospitality, or marketplace environment Strong modelling & querying capability with the ability to work independently with data, investigate issues, and answer business questions with confidence Experience building dashboards and reports that support decision-making, using BI tools such as Metabase, Grafana and Power BI or similar Strong analytical thinking with the ability to spot patterns, identify root causes, and translate findings into practical recommendations & outcomes Experience with data warehousing , and working closely with more technical data or engineering teams Experience working with digital, market and commercial data , including funnel performance, customer behaviour, campaign results, and business metrics Experience using both internal and external datasets to identify business opportunities, market trends, competitive insights, and areas of operational improvement. Ability to assess data quality and integrity , and to recognise when numbers, tracking, or reporting may be unreliable or incomplete Strong communication skills with the ability to explain complex findings clearly to non-technical stakeholders Experience applying AI tools and automation to improve reporting, analysis, and decision support, including identifying opportunities for smarter alerts, workflows, and predictive use cases. Bonus if you have: Experience working closely with Marketing or Growth teams , especially in campaign, funnel, or performance analysis Experience with Google Analytics GA4 Google BigQuery and an understanding of digital tracking, attribution, and behavioural data Experience in travel, e-commerce, hospitality, or other high-volume consumer environments Benefits Benefits Hybrid work model & flexible start times Free lunch when in office Modern offices based at the V&A Waterfront Showers on site Travel vouchers and discounts Discovery medical aid contributions & Vitality Health membership Retirement annuity contributions High-end laptops and workstation setup for both in-office and WFH Generous leave package Half-day off every month Digital nomad month (work from anywhere) Regular team events and more! We are committed to creating a diverse and inclusive workplace and encourage applications from all suitably qualified candidates, including persons with disabilities.

Title: Technical Product Analyst
Description: Who are we? Sanlam Life and Savings (SLS) is focused on serving our retail and corporate clients in South Africa and further developing our strategic advantages in the South African market. Sanlam Life and Savings consists of the following business units Retail Mass, Corporate, Risk and Savings, Glacier and various business enabling functions. The Sanlam Life and Savings Office provides strategic direction, coordination and support to the four clusters, as well as performing governance oversight that includes assurance provided by second line of defense functions in SLS, to enable us to meet our business objectives. What will you do? Sanlam Life & Savings is seeking to appoint a Technical Product Analyst within BTSS to support delivery across our PolicyOps and Fund Admin squads. The successful candidate will play a pivotal role in working with product owners and managers to define the technical requirements and to deliver high-quality, scalable, and well-documented software and solutions. This role is suited to an analytical, detail-oriented professional with strong experience in functional analysis, system design, API mapping, and data interpretation, within complex enterprise environments. Key responsibilities include: Produce functional decomposition specifications, translating business requirements into structured system-level deliverables. Develop and maintain system documentation, including: o Sequence diagrams o Activity diagrams o Use case diagrams o Data flow diagrams Create and manage user stories and supporting artefacts aligned to Agile delivery practices. Perform mapping of APIs to stored procedures within existing application landscapes. Analyze and document business rules and map them to system APIs, e.g. SARS validation rules Perform mapping and integration analysis for internal end third-party APIs, including: o Current-state (As-Is) analysis o Future-state (To-Be) integration design Conduct integration analysis for systems, ensuring alignment with architecture and regulatory requirements. Collaborate with cross-functional teams to ensure accurate translation of business needs into technical solutions. Drive and own the solution design activities, through collaboration with Technical Leads, Solution Architects and Quality Assurance Leads while ensuring alignment with architectural and integration standards. Specify and refine key business outcomes that influence the quality assurance practices across the delivery lifecycle Output Core Tasks: Develop detailed functional and system specifications supporting policy operations and fund administration processes. Produce high-quality technical diagrams and documentation using tools such as Visio and Draw.io. Drive API and data mapping activities, ensuring alignment between systems, stored procedures, and regulatory rules. Support Agile delivery cycles, including backlog refinement, sprint planning, and user story development. Ensure traceability between business requirements, system design, and delivered solutions. Facilitate collaboration between: o Business analysts o Developers o Testers o Architects Support integration and validation efforts, particularly related to third parties, amongst others and includes tax (SARS) and EBTax services. Contribute to continuous improvement of documentation standards, analysis practices, and delivery processes. What will make you successful in this role? Requirements: Qualifications: Relevant degree or diploma in: o Computer Science o Information Systems o Software Engineering o Or related discipline Certification in Business Analysis (e.g., CBAP, CCBA) or equivalent is advantageous. Experience: 5+ years’ experience in: o Systems analysis o Technical product analysis o Software delivery environments o Software Engineering design Experience in insurance, financial services, or fund administration domains is strongly preferred. Proven experience working in Agile delivery teams and delivering high quality software Experience with system integration and API-driven environments. Technical & Functional Skills Analysis & Design Strong capability in: o Functional decomposition o System analysis and design o Requirements traceability Experience producing structured technical documentation and models. API & Integration Experience mapping: o APIs to backend services or stored procedures o Business rules (e.g., SARS validation rules) to system services Exposure to integration patterns and service-based architectures. Data & SQL Strong working knowledge of SQL and relational databases. Ability to analyze data flows and validate business rules within system processes. Tools & Technologies Experience using: Visio Draw.io – for modelling and diagrams Visual Studio Code with GitHub Copilot Confluence – for documentation and collaboration Microsoft Office Suite GitHub – source control and collaboration Domain Knowledge (Advantageous) Insurance Policy Administration systems Fund Administration environments Tax-related integrations and compliance (e.g., SARS, EBTax) Regulatory-driven system design and validation rules Knowledge: Understanding of: System analysis methodologies API-driven architectures Data modelling and flow analysis Knowledge of Agile frameworks and artefacts (user stories, backlog management). Familiarity with enterprise integration and service design principles. Competencies: Strong analytical and problem-solving capability. Exceptional attention to detail and documentation quality. Ability to translate business needs into clear technical artefacts. Effective stakeholder communication across business and technical teams. High level of ownership, accountability, and delivery focus. Ability to operate in complex, regulated enterprise environments. Continuous improvement mindset and adaptability to evolving requirements. Working Relationships The role collaborates closely with: Business Analysts Developers Testers Architecture Teams Build a successful career with us We’re all about building strong, lasting relationships with our employees. We know that you have hopes for your future – your career, your personal development and of achieving great things. We pride ourselves in helping our employees to realise their worth. Through its five business clusters – Sanlam Fintech, Sanlam Life and Savings, Sanlam Investment Group, Sanlam Allianz, Santam, as well as MiWay and the Group Office – the group provides many opportunities for growth and development. Turnaround time The shortlisting process will only start once the application due date has been reached. The time taken to complete this process will depend on how far you progress and the availability of managers. Closing Date: 25 June 2026 Our commitment to transformation The Sanlam Group is committed to achieving transformation and embraces diversity. This commitment is what drives us to achieve a diverse, inclusive and equitable workplace as we believe that these are key components to ensuring a thriving and sustainable business in South Africa. The Group's Employment Equity plan and targets will be considered as part of the selection process.

Title: Utilities Analyst
Description: PURPOSE OF JOB The Utilities Analyst is responsible for ensuring accurate utility consumption tracking, billing, and cost recovery across FPG properties. The role leverages advanced data analysis, reporting tools, and industry best practices to optimize utility management, support sustainability initiatives, and provide actionable insights to the Head of Utilities and management. KEY PERFORMANCE AREAS Review and optimise utility recovery ratios to ensure accurate and efficient cost allocation. Critically analyse monthly council and tenant electricity, water, and waste data to identify discrepancies, unusual consumption, and potential cost-saving opportunities. Validate municipal and tenant invoices, ensuring correct metering, tariffs, and recoveries in line with company billing methodologies. Prepare detailed reports and dashboards to monitor utility performance, flag anomalies, and support management decision-making. Ensure all tenant, billing, municipal, and technical queries are addressed timeously, collaborating with property, finance, maintenance, and utility teams as needed. Property: tenant movements, enquiries, statement errors Finance: billing adjustments, non-payment alleviations, recoveries Utilities: meter installations, replacements, inspections, readings Maintenance: wrong readings, locating meters, logging faulty meters Council: resolving incorrect statements and obtaining credits Review accurate allocation of supply, metering, and expenses to ensure all consumption is accounted for and recovered. Identify, investigate, and escalate anomalies from utilities data to minimise negative impact on tenants and the business. Monitor unusual consumption patterns and collaborate with service providers to investigate and mitigate issues. Provide specialist and technical support to property management teams on utility-related matters. Maintain accurate records, databases, and filing systems for all properties in the group. Support utilities budgeting, forecasting, and portfolio-wide cost management. Identify improvement opportunities, implement process enhancements, and leverage technology solutions for efficiency. Monitor utilities procedures and compliance within the portfolio, keeping up-to-date with South African municipal bylaws, tariffs, Eskom electricity structures, and sustainability regulations. QUALIFICATIONS, COMPETENCIES AND SKILLS Minimum Qualification: Bachelor’s degree Diploma in Mechanical or Electrical Engineering. Experience: 5+ years’ relevant experience in utilities management within the energy services or property management sector. Technical Knowledge and Skills: Strong data analysis skills with the ability to interpret complex utility data and present findings to management. Knowledge of electricity, water, and waste management principles in multi-tenanted properties. Knowledge and understanding of solar PV systems and renewable energy concepts. Metering and verification principles, including electrical and water supply systems in buildings. Basic financial understanding: budgeting, municipal bill interpretation, and cost recovery strategies. Understanding of utility management, particularly recovery of utilities in multi-tenanted properties. Familiarity with South African municipal bylaws, tariffs, and compliance requirements is advantageous. Knowledge of electrical distribution systems and energy fundamentals is a plus. Proficiency in Microsoft Office, with strong emphasis on Excel (Intermediate or higher). Behavioural Competencies: Analytical, detail-oriented, and methodical. Strong problem-solving and decision-making ability. Excellent verbal, written, and listening communication skills. Stakeholder management and influencing skills (tenants, service providers, internal teams). Ability to work under pressure, meet deadlines, and manage time effectively. Proactive, self-motivated, and solution-focused. Collaborative, able to work across multidisciplinary teams. Other -Driver’s license Code B . Job Type: Full-time Application Question(s): Do you have a B.Eng (Electrical or Chemical) or B.Sc B.Tech in Electrical Chemical Engineering qualification? What is your notice period? Experience: relevant : 5 years (Required) Work Location: In person

Title: Data Integrations Specialist - Client Focus
Description: About the role We’re looking for a Data Integrations Specialist to join our Customer Success team at Healix International. Reporting to the Head of Customer Success, the Data Integrations Analyst sits at the intersection of people, data and systems, acting as a key link between clients, Customer Success, internal technical teams and third-party Travel Management Companies. The role ensures data integrations are delivered accurately, clearly and in line with business needs. The focus is on understanding how data should flow, coordinating stakeholders, and translating between technical detail and plain language so integrations support customer experience, operational efficiency and wider business objectives. This is a hybrid role. You do not need to be a developer, but you should be technically minded, comfortable working with data, and interested in how systems exchange information If you enjoy problem‑solving, coordinating stakeholders and translating technical detail into plain English, this may be the right role for you. As we are expecting a high number of applications, the advert may close before the published date. What we are looking for We are looking for someone who enjoys working with both people and data. You might come from customer success, implementation, operations, analytics or a junior technical background. You do not need to meet every requirement listed, but you should be curious, organised and comfortable engaging with technical detail. You are likely to: Be technically minded and interested in how systems integrate and exchange data Feel comfortable analysing structured data and investigating issues Communicate clearly with both technical and non-technical audiences Enjoy coordinating across multiple stakeholders and keeping work on track Care about accuracy, clarity and follow through Essential experience Working in a cross‑functional environment with multiple stakeholders (e.g. Customer Success, operations, implementation or delivery teams) Strong communication and collaboration skills, including translating between business needs and technical work An analytical approach to investigating issues using data Experience coordinating complex work with multiple contributors and dependencies Highly organised with strong attention to detail Intermediate Excel skills and confidence using Microsoft Office and SharePoint Desirable (but not essential) Experience with data integrations, APIs or system connections Exposure to XML, JSON or database querying tools Experience with Jira or similar workflow tools Familiarity with travel data, GDS or third‑party data providers Our values At Healix International, our values guide how we work and how we treat each other and our clients: Put people first – prioritising care and wellbeing in every decision Think independently – using judgement, expertise and integrity Earn trust – being transparent, respectful and reliable All roles at Healix are underpinned by our core competencies: Accountability, Collaboration, Communication, Problem Solving and Resilience . Interested? If you’re excited by working with data, systems and people — and want to play a meaningful role in delivering great customer experiences — we’d love to hear from you. No specific qualifications are required. About The Role What you’ll be doing Integration delivery & coordination Own the end‑to‑end coordination of data integrations, ensuring they’re delivered on time and to agreed expectations Act as the main point of contact for integration activity across clients, internal teams and external partners Lead investigations into integration issues, working with technical teams to identify root causes and agree next steps Support the rollout of new integrations, helping stakeholders understand different options, benefits and trade‑offs Business & stakeholder communication Build strong working relationships and create clarity around scope, timelines, risks and dependencies Gather integration requirements by asking insightful questions and understanding how data is used in real operational settings Communicate progress, blockers and outcomes clearly to both technical and non‑technical audiences Translate complex technical concepts into clear, business‑friendly language — and vice versa Technical coordination & data analysis Configure, test and support data integrations (e.g. GDS, API, email parsing) to ensure reliable outcomes Validate data flows and outputs before and after changes Analyse data to understand integration issues, what happened and why Monitor integration performance over time, identifying patterns, risks and improvement opportunities Document configurations, findings and behaviours clearly and share outcomes with stakeholders Compliance & risk awareness Ensure integrations align with data protection, security and compliance requirements Clearly communicate compliance considerations to support informed and responsible decision‑making Required Criteria Working in a cross‑functional environment with multiple stakeholders (e.g. Customer Success, operations, implementation or delivery teams) Strong communication and collaboration skills, including translating between business needs and technical work An analytical approach to investigating issues using data Familiarity with travel data, GDS or third‑party data providers Desired Criteria Experience with data integrations, APIs or system connections Exposure to XML, JSON or database querying tools Experience with Jira or similar workflow tools Skills Needed Salary Not disclosed

Title: Senior BI Analyst - Team Leader
Description: Senior BI Analyst - Team Leader Remote We're looking for a leader to take ownership of Business Intelligence across South Africa's number one property and automotive websites, Property24 and AutoTrader. Leading the BI team and delivering sharp commercial analysis is the heart of this role — the reporting and insight that keep the business running and that leadership, Sales, Finance, Marketing, Operations, and Product depend on every day. Underpinning that is the second pillar of the role: automation and data pipelines. Reliable analysis at this scale only works on solid foundations, so you'll own the automated pipelines and data workflows that feed reporting across both brands — making them robust, accurate, and scalable rather than manual and fragile. A defining way you'll deliver this is by building self-service systems. Rather than running every report by hand, you'll lead the team to turn routine, recurring demand into trustworthy, governed dashboards and templates that stakeholders can use themselves — freeing your analysts to focus on the genuinely hard questions where their expertise matters most. Over time, and as these foundations mature, you'll grow our data science capability — predictive pricing, anomaly detection, and ML applied to real product problems. We're deliberate about the sequence: dependable BI, automation, and self-service come first, and they're what fund and enable the more ambitious data science work. What you'll be doing + Team & leadership: Lead, develop, and manage a team of three analysts — workload allocation, quality review, and growth Set the standards for data models, definitions, and documentation the whole business relies on. Serve as the primary data partner to stakeholders across Sales, Finance, Marketing, Operations, and Product + Business intelligence & analysis (primary focus): Maintain daily automated reports: leads, listings, LPL, promoted listings, sold listings, market monitor Deliver weekly ad-hoc data requests across P24 and AutoTrader Produce monthly and quarterly commercial analyses, industry reports, and press-ready data articles Track commercial KPIs: take rate, ROS MDS, LPL per dealer, slot utilisation, dealer profitability Build and maintain dashboards in Tableau and Apache Superset; templates in Excel and Google Sheets Proactively identify data improvement opportunities and surface insights to the business + Automation & data pipelines (second focus): Write and maintain complex SQL queries and automated pipelines across P24 and AT databases Process, cleanse, and verify the integrity of data used for analysis and modelling Build recurring data workflows — revenue book, billing, finance reporting, sold data + Data science (phased): Apply predictive and statistical techniques to existing datasets — churn modelling, demand forecasting, pricing elasticity, and anomaly detection Explore ML-assisted valuation and depreciation modelling for AutoTrader Collaborate with engineering to productionise models as part of automated pipelines What we're looking for + Essential: 5+ in BI, data analytics, or data science; 1–2 years leading or mentoring others Expert-level SQL and experience building automated reporting pipelines Proficiency in at least one of Python, Tableau, Apache Superset, and Excel Strong understanding of machine learning techniques and algorithms (clustering, neural networks, boosting) Proficiency in statistical analysis, data mining, and mathematics Proven ability to manage a high-volume, multi-stakeholder request backlog independently Strong communication skills — able to translate complex data and models into clear business narrative + Desirable: Degree in Data Science, Statistics, Computer Science, or a related field Experience in online classifieds, proptech, or automotive industry Familiarity with GA4 Experience producing data for press or media-facing data publications Background in pricing or valuation modeling You'll thrive here if you are Driven to build systems and put yourself out of the business of running reports by hand. Self-motivated and comfortable working with minimal supervision A team player who takes ownership and brings a positive, proactive attitude Detail-oriented with a track record of consistently meeting deadlines Naturally curious, with an analytical mind and a genuine love for problem-solving

Title: Domain / Product owner - HR systems
Description: About Unifi Boldly different. Fiercely human. Proudly African. Unifi is redefining credit in Africa with simple, fast personal loans delivered through online, mobile and branch channels. We make life easy for our clients across Zambia, Kenya, Uganda, South Africa and Tanzania. Unifi has conviction in the African continent and its people, and our products enable our clients to achieve even more. As one of the fastest-growing lenders in Africa, we combine exceptional client service with the very best tech and data analytics. Purpose of the role We are looking for a dynamic, experienced hands on domain lead for HR systems. You will both contribute to the direction for the HR domain and personally deliver on it. You are the subject matter expert and primary driver of everything HR systems-related – setting the roadmap, shaping the processes, and doing the hands-on work to bring improvements to life. You will work closely with an HR Process and Systems Analyst, collaborating day-to-day to analyse, design, and implement solutions. Responsibilities Define and maintain the HR domain roadmap, prioritising initiatives that improve employee experience and efficiencies across all markets. Act as the subject matter expert for HR systems, roadmap and overall system and process landscape. Identify gaps and opportunities in current HR systems and processes, and build a prioritised plan to address them. Direct and prioritise the analysis work of the HR Process and Systems Analyst to analyse current-state processes, design future-state solutions, and translate requirements into actionable deliverables. Personally own and drive the configuration, improvement, and day-to-day integrity of Unifi’s HR technology stack Manage system changes end-to-end: from requirements definition and solution design through to testing, roll-out, and user adoption. Manage vendor relationships and system configurations for HR software platforms, escalating and resolving issues hands-on. Collaborate with all in-country teams to ensure solutions are fit for purpose and are making life easy. Work with the HR Process and Systems Analyst and the data team to produce dashboards Use data insights to identify trends and proactively implement system or process improvements Champion and implement employee self-service capabilities, reducing administrative burden on HR teams and improving the staff experience. Accountable for audit trails, data privacy controls, and role-based access management across all HR platforms. Own the adoption and training strategy for every HR system or process change, ensuring changes are successfully implemented and embedded across Unifi. Define adoption success criteria before go-live and track adoption metrics per country after roll-out; intervene where uptake is low. Build and maintain a network of in-country super-users champions in each market to support local roll-outs. Oversee the development of training materials and user guides (produced with the Business Process Analyst), localised where needed for each country. Ensure HR teams and end-users in every country are trained before any change goes live; no roll-out is complete until adoption is verified. Run post-implementation adoption reviews per country and feed findings back into the roadmap. Requirements Bachelor’s degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, Information Systems, or a related discipline. 6–8 years of professional experience, with at least 3 years in an HR systems, HRIS, or product owner role – ideally within a lean, hands-on environment. Proven experience personally owning or implementing core HR systems such as payroll, HRIS, ATS, or performance management platforms (not just overseeing others doing it). Strong working knowledge of HR processes across the full employee lifecycle: hire to retire. Comfortable operating as both a strategic thinker and a hands-on executor – you are equally at home defining a roadmap and configuring a system on the same day. Solid analytical skills – experience with SQL or BI tools for HR reporting is advantageous. Experience working closely with process or systems analysts; able to collaborate effectively without direct reporting lines. Strong documentation skills: able to write clear requirements documents, process maps, and user stories. Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills, with the ability to bridge HR and technical teams. High attention to detail and a meticulous approach to data quality, compliance, and process accuracy. Passion for building systems that make life easier for people while creating and exceptional user experience Proven experience driving adoption and training of system or process changes across multi-country operations Willingness to travel across the Africa as required

Title: Data Analyst
Description: Who We Are Looking For The Data Analyst researches, analyzes, and resolves technical and data-related issues, which are surfaced either via the ticketing queue or through direct interaction with clients and travel agencies. Data includes, but is not limited to, travel data, HR feed data, and site data. What You Will Work On Provide timely and effective data and system support to Crisis24 clients Investigate and troubleshoot technical and data issues using available tools, including Crisis24 applications and direct database access Identify, document, and resolve recurring issues by recognizing trends and recommending process improvements Escalate unresolved or complex issues to appropriate teams in accordance with approved escalation procedures Maintain accurate documentation of troubleshooting steps and resolutions in the Crisis24 ticketing system Ensure compliance with data security policies and protect client confidentiality, including handling Personally Identifiable Information (PII) securely Assist with general administrative duties for the data team Assist with managing data integration activities, including: Client consultations to understand customer needs and use cases Configuring products according to integration requirements Documenting integration processes and decisions Who You Will Work With This role falls within the Business Integration team, but works extensively with colleagues across the Implementations, Customer Support, Account Management, and Engineering teams. What You Will Bring Problem-Solving: Ability to analyze issues, troubleshoot effectively, and propose solutions Attention to Detail: Strong focus on data accuracy and quality assurance Communication: Clear written and verbal communication skills to interact with clients and internal teams, with the ability to clearly define technical issue and potential solutions in writing Collaboration: Ability to work in a team environment and escalate issues appropriately Time Management: Ability to handle multiple tasks, prioritize effectively, and meet deadlines Technical Proficiency: Required - Strong working knowledge of Microsoft Office (Excel, Word, Outlook) Preferred – exposure to SQL and other database tools Preferred – exposure to ticketing systems such as Jira Capability to effectively coordinate and lead discussions with internal and external clients travel agencies Attention to detail is a must Ability to multitask, organize, and document Self-starter. Proactive and able to find and complete meaningful tasks in the absence of client demand and without management directive Required: 2 years+ experience working with data (reporting, analysis, or insights) Relevant qualification in Data, IT, Statistics, Business, or similar (preferred) Proficiency in Excel (pivot tables, formulas) Strong communication skills and the ability to present findings clearly Problem-solving ability and a structured approach to work

Title: Customer Success Reporting Analyst
Description: Who we are: Mama Money is a growth-stage fintech on a mission to improve the financial lives of migrants and underserved communities across Africa and beyond. What started in 2015 as a cross-border payments service has grown into a multi-product financial platform. We give people the tools to send, save, and manage money — on their own terms, at a socially fair price. Mama HQ is in beautiful Cape Town, South Africa. We're 150 people strong (and counting), representing 19 countries across Africa, Asia, the UK, and Europe. From remittances to a growing range of financial products, everything we build is in service of people who deserve better. Our culture reflects that same commitment. We look after our team the way we want them to look after our customers. "Just be lekker!" means we trust our talented, diverse people to do what's right and make it happen — simply and with heart. We've earned a few accolades, built lasting partnerships, and grown our reach in ways we're proud of. We've had good times and tough times, but our focus has never shifted. People over profit, always. We're looking for a Customer Success Reporting Analyst to help us turn data into actionable insights that improve customer experience and operational performance. In this role, you'll be responsible for reporting, dashboard development, workforce management support, Zendesk administration, and identifying opportunities to improve processes through data, and automation. As our Customer Success Reporting Analyst you will: Develop practical tracking processes, governance routines, and performance reporting. Manage and prioritise requests based on business value, urgency, dependencies, and team capacity. Build dashboards, consolidate information, and create clear reports to support decision-making. Support end-to-end Zendesk implementations, including setup, configuration, and deployment. Design and improve ticket workflows, routing rules, SLAs, automations, triggers, macros, and views. Configure and manage customer support channels, including voice, email, chat, and messaging. Review and optimise the Zendesk platform to improve efficiency, customer satisfaction, and operational costs. Define AI use cases, guidelines, and escalation processes with key stakeholders. Monitor AI performance and continuously improve it using feedback and reporting. Ensure workforce management (WFM) systems are updated accurately and on time. Monitor contact centre operations to maintain appropriate staffing levels. Produce and analyse intraday reports to track performance and identify trends or issues. About you: Strong analytical skills with the ability to turn data into meaningful insights and recommendations. Highly organised and detail-oriented, with the ability to manage multiple priorities and meet deadlines. Experienced in working with reporting tools, dashboards, and large data sets to identify trends and opportunities. Strong communication and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to present information clearly and effectively. Proactive, adaptable, and committed to continuous improvement, accuracy, and delivering high-quality work. Why Mama? At Mama Money, we build products that help people do more with their money, from sending money home, to saving, to accessing money in ways that feel safe & simple. The work we do matters because it helps real people in real ways every day. We care about making life easier for our customers. We’re not afraid to give things a go, and we take ownership of what we do. We keep learning, support each other, and stay focused on what matters most. If you’re looking for meaningful work and a team that cares about what they’re building, Mama Money could be the right place for you. Mama’s values: Making it easier for customers Give it a go Just own it NB: Applications only open to candidates in Cape Town, or candidates who are actively looking to relocate at their own expense. We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Title: Business Intelligence Analyst
Description: Title: Business Intelligence Analyst Location: Head Office, Wynberg Department: Information Technology Marketing Reports to: IT Executive The Business Intelligence (BI) Analyst is responsible for transforming enterprise data into actionable insights that support operational, commercial, and strategic decision-making across Queenspark. Dynamics 365 application · Retail industry and e-commerce exposure Key Responsibilities Customer Data and CRM · Own and manage customer data flows between the data warehouse and the business · Assist with the maintenance of customer segments based on purchase behavior, engagement history and lifetime value · Support CRM communication strategies across email, SMS, WhatsApp and loyalty communications with data extracts · Monitor data quality and identify inaccuracies or gaps within customer information · Work with IT and external partners to improve data integrity and reporting summaries that non-technical stakeholders can understand · Flag underperforming areas and provide data backed recommendations · Support the planning and buying teams with product and customer trend analysis Dashboards and Reporting Tools · Assist in maintaining existing dashboards and reports using tools such as Power BI, Google Analytics and Excel · Ensure recurring reports are accurate, up to date and distributed on schedule · Work with IT and external partners to improve reporting accessibility and automation for · Omnichannel Data Support · Maintain an understanding of data flows and Omnichannel customer touchpoints · Assist with data integration and accuracy initiatives Skills and Behavioural Competencies · SQL and data analysis · MS Office, in particular advanced MS Excel (external data sources, pivot tables, etc.) · Understand large data sets (cleaning, scrubbing, organizing and validating) · Ability to understand retail operations, customer behaviour, and commercial drivers · Confident in engaging with cross-functional teams and able to build strong, trusted working relationships · Ability to interpret complex data, identify trends, root causes, and provide BI tools, trends, and best practices · Comfortable working in a fast-paced retail environment with evolving priorities and changing business requirements · Works effectively across business teams, process: Interested candidates to complete the Internal Application form(Internal Applicants) and email with an updated CV to recruitment@queenspark.com by no later than Wednesday, 17 June 2025. At Queenspark, we value passion, professionalism, integrity, and customer focus. You’ll be part of a team that thrives on solving problems, supporting colleagues, and creating great experiences for both staff and customers. Work Location: In person

Title: Conflict Analyst
Description: Welcome to Frontline Managed Services® – where innovation, technology, and efficiency converge to redefine the landscape of IT, Financial, and Administrative Managed Services for legal and professional service firms. As pioneers in the industry, we are driven by a relentless commitment to excellence. Join Our Team and Be a Catalyst for Change! We don't just follow industry standards; we set them. Our dynamic environment thrives on pushing boundaries and embracing challenges. We are more than a workplace; we are a community of forward-thinkers dedicated to shaping the future. Position Overview You will safeguard the firm’s risk posture and enable seamless client intake by conducting conflicts checks, corporate party research, and issue analysis that attorneys can trust. Working across Practice, Conflicts, and Administrative teams, you’ll turn complex information into clear, decision-ready summaries—on time, with discretion, and to a high standard of accuracy. Schedule: Monday through Friday - 3 PM to 12 AM SAST Location: In-office position based in Cape Town Compensation: Starting pay ranges from $300,000 - $600,000k Rand annually. What You’ll Do Conflicts intake & clearance Run new-matter and new-party conflict searches using firm databases and approved sources; deliver concise, cite-backed summaries within agreed SLAs. Identify potential conflicts and related parties; escalate and document waivers ethical screens as required. Corporate & party research Perform corporate genealogy and background checks using commercial providers and public records; validate entity status, ownership, and key relationships. Produce written research briefs that surface risks, discrepancies, and data gaps, with recommendations for disposition. Quality, accuracy, and recordkeeping Verify and reconcile data across systems; correct posting computation errors and maintain audit-ready documentation of searches, sources, and decisions. Client service & communication Serve as a responsive point of contact for attorneys, legal assistants, and admin teams; acknowledge requests promptly and meet or exceed response SLAs. Translate findings into plain-English summaries that support quick attorney decision-making. Process improvement & special projects Track recurring exceptions, propose search templates or checklists, and help reduce rework turnaround time over time. Support special projects as assigned. What You Bring Legal degree required. Possesses a minimum of 2 years of litigation experience, or 3+ years of experience performing conflicts analysis or related risk compliance functions. Demonstrated ability to read and understand legal documents, parties, and events (pleadings, orders, corporate filings). Proven analytical research capability and skill in summarizing findings with clear risk flags. Discretion and sound judgment handling highly confidential information. Excellent interpersonal, verbal, and written communication skills; builds effective relationships across teams. Strong organization and time management; thrives in a fast-paced, deadline-driven setting. Technically adept: proficient with Microsoft Office (Excel, Word, Outlook) and comfortable learning firm databases and research tools. Meticulous attention to detail and commitment to client service. Not Sure You Meet Every Requirement? We know that great candidates may not match every qualification listed. If you’re excited about the role and believe you could be a strong fit, we encourage you to apply. We value potential and a growth mindset as much as experience. Join Us At Frontline Managed Services, we celebrate different backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives. We are committed to building a team that reflects the clients and communities we serve. “We are an equal opportunity employer. All applicants will be considered for employment without attention to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, veteran or disability status.”

Title: Paid Media Analyst
Description: Description CleverProfits is a financial services firm that helps online businesses in the US build highly profitable and sustainable businesses. They offer profit-focused services to a variety of clients in different industries, including digital and social media marketing agencies, web design and software development agencies, coaches and course creators, consultants, affiliate and network marketers, influencers and streamers, SaaS and E-commerce businesses. CleverProfits uses the latest cloud platforms to serve businesses remotely from anywhere in the world. Why work with CleverProfits? As a start-up, we’re a young dynamic team that puts our people first. We provide our team with opportunities for career advancement and professional development within a rapidly growing company. We work hard, we help each other succeed, and it is our shared mission to provide a higher level of service to our clients. We use our core values of DIY Mentality, Productive Communication, Performance Accountability, Quirky, and Grit to guide us to be better people, teammates, and professionals. About the Role We’re looking for a Paid Media Analyst to join our growing marketing team and help build CleverProfits’ paid media department from the ground up. If you live and breathe ad data, find yourself obsessing over performance metrics, and get a genuine thrill from optimising every dollar of ad spend, this role was made for you. You’ll report directly to the Marketing Manager and work primarily within the Meta Ads ecosystem, managing and optimising campaigns across a diverse portfolio of clients. As an early hire in a newly established department, the growth potential here is significant, both in the scope of your responsibilities and in your career trajectory. Key Responsibilities Manage and optimise Meta Ads campaigns (Facebook & Instagram) across multiple client accounts Analyse ad performance data to uncover trends, identify opportunities, and diagnose underperformance Strategically allocate and manage ad spend budgets to maximise return on investment Build, test, and iterate on ad creatives, audiences, targeting strategies, and campaign structures Produce clear, data-driven performance reports with actionable insights and recommendations Monitor Meta platform updates, algorithm changes, and industry best practices, and adapt strategies accordingly Collaborate closely with the Marketing Manager to align paid media activity with broader client and business goals Contribute to the development of paid media processes, frameworks, and playbooks as the department scales Qualifications Required: 1–2 years of hands-on experience managing paid media campaigns (agency or in-house) Proven experience running and optimising Meta Ads (Facebook and Instagram) campaigns Strong analytical mindset with a data-driven approach to decision making and budget management Ability to interpret performance data and translate it into clear, actionable recommendations High attention to detail and the ability to manage multiple campaigns and accounts simultaneously Strong written and verbal communication skills A proactive, self-starter attitude — you don’t wait to be told what to improve Advantageous: Experience with Google Ads (Search, Display, Performance Max, or Shopping) Working knowledge of SEO principles and how paid and organic strategies complement each other Familiarity with analytics platforms such as Google Analytics 4 and Meta Business Suite Experience with A B testing methodologies and conversion rate optimisation Benefits: Medical Aid Subsidy Local Travel Benefit Hybrid Work Model Paid Maternity and Parental Leave Exposure to the US market Be a key part of building a marketing department for a strategic advisory firm from the ground up in an innovative and rapidly growing niche Work alongside young, smart, talented people who will push you and help you grow Build experience working with a wide range of businesses and industries, while developing skills in leading and managing change Learn a valuable breadth of skills that span across the entire business cycle

Title: Supply Chain Process Analyst

To provide meaningful business information solutions to the Supply Chain and Logistics Business that is aligned with the achievement of the strategic goals and scorecards. At Woolworths, you'll be part of a forward-thinking team committed to innovation and operational excellence, working in a collaborative environment that values your expertise and growth. Take this opportunity to contribute to a leading company renowned for its commitment to quality, integrity, and customer focus. Apply now to make an impact and grow your career with Woolworths. Minimum Requirements 3-year commercial or information technology degree Project management life cycle knowledge 3 - 5 years relevant experience within a Retail operations environment Systems development life cycle experience Advanced Excel (with experience using graphs, pivots etc.) 3 or more years’ proven experience in the field of Business Intelligence, preferably in a retail environment. Ability to combine and analyse data from various sources and produce visual and or exception based output that portrays answers to business questions Ability to draw insights from data analytics, summarize findings and make recommendations for improvement The ability to turn data and or insights into effective BI solutions Ability to work with large volumes of data Data Visualization tools experience preferable (i.e.,Tableau Qlikview) Duties and Responsibilities Work with the relevant stakeholders to understand the requirements, facilitate solution exploration, develop the Business Requirement Specification(s) and obtain consensus Develop reporting solutions by using relevant tools and through an interactive process develop and refine reports Proactively identify and communicate impacts, risks and issues Provide input into any system, people and process specifications Ensure the integrity of the solution in accordance with the business requirements Plan, drive and execute functional testing and business sign-off Review training documentation and participate in project training Proactively identify opportunities to improve and streamline business processes Ensure Supply Chain and Logistics Business Information needs are catered for by creating dynamic reports and data visualizations that provide insights to the Supply Chain and Logistics user community Engage with business and IT stakeholders to focus on continuous improvement of information in terms of relevance, quality and speed of delivery Timeous and accurate production of Supply Chain and Logistics reports which includes analysis, trends and interpretation Service ad-hoc requests for information within the required timeframe and specification, with a focus on translating ad-hoc solutions into effective, sustainable BI solutions that service a broader need Support the business with report and data queries and manage end-to-end resolution of issues by engaging with all stakeholders Ability to work on own, as well as a good team-member Attention to detail Ability to work in fast-changing environment (i.e., flexibility and adaptability) Strong communication skills and ability to
