improvement mindset Process design and documentation skills Business requirement analysis skills

Title: BUSINESS ANALYST (INDUSTRIAL ENGINEER)
Description: Listing reference: cartr_000815 Listing status: Online Apply by: 31 July 2026 Position summary Industry: IT & Internet Job category: Others: Transport and Logistics Location: Cape Town Contract: Permanent EE position: No Introduction Picup, a last-mile delivery and technology-focused company, powered by a scalable crowd-sourced driver network, seeks an analytical Industrial Engineer who combines process optimization expertise with data analysis capabilities to drive improvements in our last-mile delivery operations. This role bridges operational excellence with data-driven decision making by applying industrial engineering to last-mile delivery. Job description Key Responsibilities: Process Analysis & Optimization: Apply industrial engineering principles to analyse and optimize delivery operations. Use insights and results derived from historical delivery data to optimize future operations. Incorporate geospatial and operational simulation results into recommendations. Identify correlations between different operational variables and performance outcomes. Monitor and analyse KPIs related to delivery performance, fleet utilization, and operational costs. Develop deep operational knowledge of our delivery platforms and systems to inform improvement recommendations. Design and maintain methodology of a Client Prioritisation Scoring Matrix system. Directly contribute to initiatives in modelling feasibility, risks, impact and suggest recommendations. Stay current on last-mile delivery and technology trends to benchmark and inform strategies. Investigate internal processes to identify areas of waste and ways of improving. Data Analysis & Insights: Collaborate with Data and technology stakeholders to gather operational data. Write SQL queries to extract operational and spatial data for analysis. Perform exploratory data analysis to identify patterns and trends in delivery performance. Track and evaluate ROI (Return On Investment) of initiatives and new processes. Generate reports for process-level performance, KPI’s, SLA’s and success metrics. Stakeholder Management & Communication: Prepare and present analytical findings and recommendations to leadership and other stakeholders. Collaborate with operations teams to implement process improvements. Collaborate with the Data and Data Engineering stakeholders to ensure data accessibility. Prepare and maintain business proposals, process documentation, and analytical methodologies. Manage multiple projects simultaneously while meeting deadlines. Communicate complex analytical findings to non-technical stakeholders. Technical Implementation: Develop process simulation models, cost-benefit models and capacity models. Create and maintain process flow diagrams, model documentation, and documentation for existing and proposed workflows. Support the integration of analytical findings into operational procedures. Ensure compliance with data privacy regulations, logistics standards and safety protocols. Ad Hoc: Support capacity planning and resource allocation initiatives. Ad hoc analysis, queries, and business support Minimum requirements Required Qualifications: Bachelor's degree in Industrial Engineering 5-7 years of experience in process improvement or operations analysis. A big plus: Experience is combination of Supply Chain Logistics and Startup Technology related businesses Competencies: Strong analytical and structured problem-solving skills Proven process and systems modelling experience Excellent time management and prioritization skills Proficiency in data extraction and analysis using Microsoft Excel Google Sheets and SQL Extensive experience with process mapping and optimization Basic understanding of statistical analysis principles Visualise complex information using graphs and other visualisations Good understanding of Programming and Software Engineering concepts Excellent client engagement and expectation management skills Technical Skills: Process mapping and optimization tools e.g. Visio, Lucidchart - intermediate to advanced Microsoft Excel Google Sheets - intermediate to advanced SQL (Structured Query Language) - basic to intermediate Data visualization tools e.g. Looker Studio, Tableau, Power BI - basic A Plus: GIS (Geographic Information System) experience e.g. ArcGIS, QGIS - basic

Title: Manager, Grade 1: Spatial Analyst
Description: Manager, Grade 1: Spatial Analyst Salary Level 11 Fixed-Term Contract of Employment for five (5) years All-inclusive remuneration package of R 932 292.00 per annum Kirstenbosch National Botanical Garden, Cape Town Biodiversity Information & Policy Advice Reference Number: Eco-DRR 2026 0010 The South African National Biodiversity Institute (SANBI) is looking for a self-motivated and professional individual to oversee and provide advanced spatial analysis and GIS technical support across the three components of the Eco-DRR project, ensuring the delivery of high-quality geospatial outputs that inform planning, reporting, and decision-making. This position requires a degree in Environmental Science, Geoinformatics, Natural Sciences, Geography, Ecology, or equivalent qualification at NQF 7. A relevant post-graduate qualification at NQF 8 will be an added advantage. This position also requires 8 years of work experience in the relevant field, 4 years of which should be in spatial analysis or first line of management of GIS projects. The following competencies are required for the candidate to be successful in this position: Advanced knowledge and demonstrated experience in GIS, spatial analysis including data collection, management and flow, and environmental spatial planning. Extensive experience in leading and managing spatial projects. Proven experience in the development and application of spatial decision-support tools, vulnerability and risk mapping, landscape and ecosystem assessments, and spatial prioritization approaches. Demonstrated ability to interpret and translate complex spatial information into accessible technical products, maps, reports, and science communication outputs to support planning, decision-making, monitoring, evaluation, learning, and reporting processes. Strong leadership and ability to supervise, mentor, and build the technical capacity of employees and project partners in spatial planning and GIS applications. Expected to have advanced knowledge of compliance and risk management techniques and effective people, budget and financial management skills. South African Council for Natural Scientific Profession (SACNASP) or Plato registration will be advantageous and the candidate must be in possession of a valid driver’s licence with one year practical driving experience. Key Performance Responsibilities include: Lead the sourcing, validation and governance of high-quality spatial datasets for the Eco-DRR project; Guide and undertake advanced spatial analyses for ecological infrastructure mapping and site prioritization across target districts; Coordinate spatial inputs across partners to ensure aligned, integrated, and consistent GIS outputs; Oversee capacity development in spatial analysis, ecological infrastructure mapping and GIS data management; and Manage the interpretation of spatial information into accessible knowledge products for diverse audience ........................................................................................................................................................... Please Note: 1) Only online applications will be accepted and applications must be accompanied by a CV together with a letter motivating why the applicant should be favourably considered for this position, and certified qualifications confirming that the minimum qualifications are met (applicants lacking evidence of meeting minimum qualifications and relevant experience will not be considered). 2) A competency test will be conducted as part of the selection process. 3) Applicants must also provide the full names, addresses and telephone numbers and if possible e-mail addresses of at least three referees. 4) Failure to submit the requested information and reference number shall result in your application not being considered. Personal Information will be processed by SANBI for purposes of assessing your suitability for the advertised position. SANBI may also share your personal information including special personal information with service providers or third parties, both within the Republic of South Africa and in other jurisdictions, to carry out verification of qualifications and other necessary documents, background and reference checks, competency assessment tests, and criminal record checks. In this regard, by virtue of your application, you acknowledge that SANBI’s authorised verification agent(s) and service providers will access your personal information including special personal information to conduct background screening, verification of qualifications and other necessary documents, reference checks, competency assessment tests, and criminal record checks. For more information, kindly refer to SANBI’s website. Persons with disabilities are encouraged to apply. SANBI reserves the right not to fill this position. All applications will be considered with the understanding that, in terms of the SANBI Employment Equity Plan, preference will be given to candidates from the designated groups. Contact Ms Seipati Nku on Tel: +27 (0)12 3392749 for any queries for purposes of clarity. Applications consisting of the completed application form, motivation letter, a concise CV, and certified qualifications are to be submitted via the online portal. Candidates are expected to be available for selection interviews on a date and time as determined by SANBI. If no response has been received within 30 days of the closing date, candidates must consider their applications unsuccessful.

Title: Integration Engineer/Developer
Description: Why Join Cape Union Mart? Cape Union Mart is South Africa’s leading outdoor, fashion, and lifestyle retailer, with a proud heritage of delivering exceptional quality and service for over 90 years. Our IT Division is at the forefront of digital transformation, driving innovation and efficiency across all business systems. As part of our dynamic and collaborative IT team, you will play a crucial role in shaping the future of our enterprise solutions, working on cutting-edge technology, and contributing to impactful projects in a fast-paced retail environment. If you’re passionate about technology, problem-solving, and making a difference, Cape Union Mart is the perfect place to grow your career! Job Purpose: The Integration Engineer will be responsible for designing, configuring, and maintaining Oracle Integration Cloud (OIC) solutions that enable seamless integration between third-party systems and Oracle Cloud. Additionally, the candidate will play a key role in developing and maintaining Oracle APEX applications, ensuring high-performance, scalable, and secure web-based solutions that support business needs. The role requires strong technical expertise in OIC, API integrations, middleware technologies, and Oracle APEX development. The role includes out-of-hours support for critical systems as part of a standby rotation. Key Responsibilities: Develop and configure Oracle Integration Cloud (OIC) solutions to handle transformations and orchestrations between third-party systems and Oracle Cloud. Design, develop, and maintain Oracle APEX applications, ensuring they align with business requirements and deliver a seamless user experience. Work with product owners and technical leads to understand integration and application development requirements, providing scalable solutions. Ensure timely delivery of assigned development tasks while adhering to quality standards and deadlines. Collaborate with QA teams, analysts, and other stakeholders to analyze requirements, develop user stories, and manage backlog items. Provide technical expertise in system integrations, API development, and middleware solutions. Monitor, troubleshoot, and optimize integration solutions to enhance performance and scalability. Participate in Agile development processes, including standup meetings, sprint planning, and reviews. Build prototypes and proof-of-concepts (POCs) to explore new integration and application solutions. Required Technical and Professional Expertise: Experience: Minimum 2 years of relevant experience in Oracle Integration Cloud Services (OIC). Minimum 2 years of experience in Oracle APEX for web-based application development. Technical Skills: API integrations and REST SOAP web services. Data transformation and orchestration between multiple systems. Middleware technologies and best practices. Strong SQL and PL SQL programming expertise. Other programming language eg: C would be advantageous. Experience in developing interactive, responsive, and user-friendly web applications using Oracle APEX. Building dynamic reports, charts, dashboards, and forms in APEX. JavaScript, HTML, and CSS knowledge for front-end enhancements. Authentication and security best practices in APEX applications. Hands-on experience in integration development, API design, and cloud-based solutions. Experience working with JSON, XML, and SQL databases for integration and APEX applications. Familiarity with CI CD pipelines and DevOps best practices for integration and application deployment. Professional Competencies: Ability to work in an agile environment and deliver within tight deadlines. Strong analytical and problem-solving skills. Excellent communication skills to collaborate with cross-functional teams. Self-motivated and capable of working independently while maintaining alignment with team objectives. Preferred Qualifications: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field. Certifications in Oracle Integration Cloud Services (OICS), Oracle APEX Development, or Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI). Additional Information: This is a permanent full-time position based at Cape Union Mart’s IT Division. Occasional travel may be required based on project needs. The role requires the ability to work in a fast-paced retail environment with evolving business requirements. Please note: The Cape Union Mart Group is committed to transformation. Appointments and promotions will be made based on candidates who best meet the requirements for the position. Candidates that enhance the diversity of the team will be given preference, in line with our Employment Equity plan.

Title: Insight Analyst (Professional, BI Specialist (TCF))
Description: JOB PURPOSE To support the development and implementation of service insights on strategic client accounts, with a strong emphasis on practical and quantifiable solutions derived from intelligent analytical insights. This role plays a key part in differentiating the organization in the outsourced contact center market through applied analytics. By leveraging structured analytical methodologies, the Insights Analyst drives, creates, and supports the implementation of measurable, data-driven operational solutions that enhance business performance and efficiency ACCOUNTABILTIES & DELIVERABLES Accountabilities & Deliverables Data Analysis & Insights Generation Analyze large datasets to identify trends, patterns, and key business insights. Develop actionable insights that drive business decisions and improve operational performance. Use statistical techniques, predictive modeling, and data mining to enhance reporting accuracy. Reporting & Visualization Design and maintain dashboards, reports, and scorecards using tools like SQL, Power BI, Tableau, or Excel . Ensure data integrity and accuracy in reporting, aligning with business needs. Present insights in a clear and compelling manner to both technical and non-technical stakeholders. Business Problem Solving Identify root causes of business challenges using data-driven approaches. Recommend data-backed solutions to optimize business processes, efficiency, and customer experience. Work cross-functionally with teams to implement insights-driven strategies. Performance Tracking & Continuous Improvement Monitor key performance indicators (KPIs) and provide insights for performance enhancement. Support the development of forecasting models to aid decision-making. Continuously refine data methodologies to improve the accuracy of insights and reporting. Stakeholder Engagement & Collaboration Engage with key stakeholders to understand data requirements and business objectives. Communicate insights effectively to senior management and operational teams. Act as a strategic partner by providing data-driven recommendations for business growth. PERSON SPECIFICIATION 2+ years of experience in data analysis, insights, or a similar analytical role. Proficient in data visualization tools like Power BI, Tableau, or Looker. Knowledge of SQL for querying databases. Proven understanding of fundamental statistical analytical techniques (Process Analytical skills such as Six Sigma or Prince 2 would be advantageous) Experience with statistical programming languages such as Python or R is a plus. Excellent communication and storytelling skills to convey data insights to technical and non-technical audiences. Strong problem-solving skills and attention to detail. Ability to develop practical solutions based on outputs of data Familiarity with predictive analytics and machine learning techniques is a bonus. Microsoft application proficiency; in Excel and PowerPoint Understanding of our client’s systems, processes and best practice

Title: Investment Guidelines Analyst (Contractor)
Description: Purpose of the position A vacancy has arisen for an Investment Guidelines Analyst on a 12-month contract basis. The individual will be playing a key role in maintaining investment restrictions, performing pre-trade compliance analysis, and monitoring post-trade breaches across client portfolios. Key responsibilities of the position The successful candidate will fulfil the following primary functions: o Performing pre-trade compliance analysis for SA funds and portfolios, ensuring adherence to client mandates and regulatory requirements; o Interpreting client guidelines, regulatory rules, and internal restrictions - code these into CRIMS and equivalent order management platforms, ensuring alignment with mandate terms; o Being responsible for daily monitoring of post-trade compliance breaches and reporting these to the relevant departments - a core function of the role; o Reviewing, interpreting and operationalising client investment mandates, ensuring guidelines and restrictions are accurately captured and maintained in the order management system; o Quality-checking and peer reviewing coded rules to ensure accuracy and completeness; o Engaging with portfolio managers, traders and operations staff to evaluate and resolve pre-trade queries, post-trade queries and breaches; o Continually reviewing and improving processes to reduce risk and improve efficiency, contributing to a culture of continuous improvement; o Assisting the team with other BAU work and ad hoc tasks and projects, including the current CRIMS compliance implementation; o Compiling operational and monitoring reports for internal stakeholders, portfolio managers, and clients where required; o Addressing ad hoc queries from portfolio management, operations, and client-facing teams relating to compliance, mandate interpretation, and operational processes; and o Navigating and extracting information from back-office and portfolio administration systems to investigate non-adherence incidents and operational exceptions. Knowledge and experience required Skills, professional qualifications and experience: o Bachelor’s Degree, preferably BCom or equivalent; o A minimum of 2–3 years’ relevant working experience within an investment or asset management business, with knowledge of fixed income instruments, regulatory restrictions, and portfolio administration; o Detailed working knowledge of Charles River (CRD), thinkFolio, CRIMS, HiPortfolio or similar compliance and portfolio management systems will be highly advantageous; and o Computer literacy across the Microsoft suite; exposure to investment administration systems and data tools will be an advantage. Key behaviours and competencies: o Analytical and critical thinking with the ability to analyse, interpret and assimilate complex investment and regulatory information. o Excellent communication skills (both written and verbal). o Ability to build and maintain meaningful relationships across front and middle office stakeholders. o Client-focused and collaborative approach, with high conviction and comfort sharing opinions. o Quality focus and strong attention to detail. o Ability to work independently and as part of a team. o Takes responsibility for quality of work and output. o Pro-active can-do approach. o Multi-tasking and time management skills given business needs. o Ability to deliver under pressure. Recruitment process Selected candidates will need to attend a series of competency-based interviews activities and a psychometric assessment.

Title: FinOps Analyst
Description: Who we are Yoco was founded in 2015, and we’ve been breaking down barriers, unlocking economic opportunities and opening commerce for people to thrive ever since. We’re the payments provider for over 200,000 self-employed, and process over US$3 billion annually. We have ±350 team members globally, all with a bias for boldness and a passion for simple, progressive solutions. We believe in uniting different people to solve hard problems, together. Our mission of making society more equal requires a variety of people, with different backgrounds and points of view, to keep building solutions that make life easier for emerging businesses. We’re growing fast. With growth comes compelling challenges. If you’re an ambitious problem-solver, with big ideas, a passion for doing good, bright, grounded and courageous; you’re likely to thrive at Yoco. We don’t stop pushing. We break things to rebuild. We challenge ourselves and each other. We’re constantly evolving - and we’re doing it fast. Grow With Us. About the Cloud Engineering team The Cloud Engineering team provides the platforms, tooling, and operational foundations that enable Yoco’s engineering teams to build, deploy, and operate reliable services at scale. We manage and evolve our cloud environments across AWS, Azure, and GCP, ensuring our infrastructure remains secure, performant, resilient, and cost-effective. As Yoco continues to scale, cloud and SaaS spend have become increasingly important strategic levers. The Cloud Engineering team plays a key role in ensuring that technology investments deliver measurable business value while enabling engineering teams to move quickly and confidently. About the role As a FinOps Analyst, you will be responsible for ensuring Yoco derives maximum value from every rand spent across our cloud and SaaS ecosystem. Reporting to the Cloud Operations team within Cloud Engineering, you will serve as the bridge between Engineering, Finance, and Procurement, bringing visibility, accountability, and optimisation to technology spend across AWS, Azure, GCP, and our core SaaS platforms. You will own and drive the FinOps lifecycle across Cloud Engineering spend and associated technology platforms by improving cost transparency, identifying optimisation opportunities, governing SaaS utilisation, and partnering with engineering teams to embed cost-conscious decision-making into day-to-day operations. This role is ideal for someone with a strong cloud infrastructure background who has developed a passion for cloud economics, cost optimisation, and using data to drive operational and commercial outcomes. Success in this role requires a combination of analytical rigour, technical credibility, commercial awareness, and the ability to influence stakeholders without formal authority. What you will be doing Develop and maintain cost visibility dashboards that provide accurate allocation of cloud and SaaS spend across departments, products, and projects. Monitor, analyse, and optimise spend across AWS, Azure, and GCP through effective management of Reserved Instances, Savings Plans, Committed Use Discounts, and other optimisation mechanisms. Identify, quantify, and drive cost-saving opportunities by partnering with engineering teams on initiatives such as rightsizing, storage optimisation, and workload efficiency improvements. Govern SaaS utilisation across key platforms, ensuring licensing remains aligned to business needs and eliminating unnecessary spend. Build strong relationships with Engineering, Finance, and Procurement stakeholders to establish FinOps best practices and operational processes. Lead commercial discussions with technology vendors to negotiate favourable pricing, contract terms, and enterprise agreements. Investigate anomalies, proactively detect cost spikes, and provide meaningful recommendations to mitigate financial risk. Deliver regular reporting and insights to leadership, enabling informed decision-making around technology investment and cloud consumption. About you You have 3–8 years of experience in FinOps, Cloud Operations, Cloud Engineering, DevOps, Platform Engineering, or a closely related field. You have hands-on experience managing and optimising cloud spend within AWS and ideally across Azure and GCP. You can demonstrate measurable cost optimisation outcomes and clearly articulate the actions taken to achieve them. You have a strong understanding of cloud pricing models, discount structures, and optimisation techniques across major cloud providers. You are comfortable interrogating large datasets and can independently build analyses using SQL, spreadsheets, billing exports, or other analytical tools. You understand cloud infrastructure concepts and can engage credibly with engineers on topics such as compute, storage, networking, and workload optimisation. You have experience managing SaaS licensing, utilisation, renewals, and vendor relationships. You are comfortable negotiating commercial agreements and working with procurement stakeholders. You are highly collaborative and know how to influence priorities across multiple teams without relying on formal authority. You enjoy solving complex problems, challenging assumptions, and turning data into actionable business outcomes. Bonus points FinOps Certified Practitioner (FOCP) or FinOps Certified Professional certification. Experience operating within FinTech, SaaS, cloud-native, or high-growth technology environments. Experience managing multi-million-rand cloud budgets. Experience consolidating SaaS tooling into enterprise-wide commercial agreements. The people we’re looking for We’re looking for people who want to grow and have a thirst for learning. And as Yoco grows, we hope they stay with us for the long-term. Building solutions for a more equal society is a daunting task - and it’s not for everyone. We never stop pushing, we break things to rebuild, and we challenge ourselves and our teammates. We start over, we constantly evolve - and we do it fast. We know that it’s just the right kind of meaningful madness for our kind of visionary human. So, who are you? You’re someone who resonates with our mission and our values, and you’re relentlessly effective in your execution. You’re a curious problem-solver with a passion for doing good. You’re bright, grounded, experimental and bold. You play open cards and get stuck in. You’re not afraid of change. You close the loop. At Yoco, we laugh, embrace each other’s quirks, and support one another’s growth, all while staying authentic. If this sounds like your kind of challenge, apply below and come grow with us. We encourage applicants from diverse backgrounds to apply and ask that you please send your application in English and help us reduce unconscious bias by leaving out your picture, age, address, and other unnecessary information in your CV.

Title: Databricks Data Engineer
Description: Position: Data Engineer - Intermediate (Hybrid Pinelands, Cape Town) Confluent Services, South Africa’s largest and multi-award winning debt management company, has a position available for a Data Engineer. This individual will join the Data Team and will be accountable for data engineering design, data ingestion and support across on-premise and cloud-based platforms. What’s in it for you? Being South Africa’s largest company of its kind, Confluent is continuously growing, with no signs of stopping. At Confluent, we build on current-generation platforms like Databricks and Microsoft Fabric, giving you hands-on exposure to tools and patterns that are shaping the future of data engineering. This is not a legacy maintenance role, you’ll be involved in designing, building, and evolving data solutions from the ground up, with real room for creativity and engineering judgement. You’ll work alongside a team of high caliber senior data engineers, where knowledge sharing is part of how we operate. Technical discussions are encouraged, opinions are respected, and decisions are driven by merit rather than hierarchy. Our team is actively involved in AI and machine learning initiatives, giving you exposure beyond traditional data pipelines. We value clean architecture, thoughtful design, and creativity. You will have the opportunity to influence how things are built, contribute to architectural decisions, and help shape the evolution of our data platform. Culturally, we are a diverse, respectful, and collaborative team. We invest in people, support continuous learning, and create an environment where engineers can grow both technically and professionally. Whether you want to deepen your engineering expertise or broaden your impact across the business, you’ll find room to do so here. Beyond the work itself, Confluent offers a stable, growing environment where the data you build helps real South Africans improve their financial outcomes, giving purpose and impact to the systems you design. Other key benefits : We have a vibrant office in a lively part of the city, meaning you have easy access to stores and outlets if you need. We offer company benefits – medical aid, retirement annuity, Bursaries and Insurance We have a culture of growth, meaning you have access to move both upwards or across in the business if you want to. Requirements Candidates must be able to demonstrate: 3 years experience as a data engineer or in a similar role, designing and implementing data pipelines, data integration and ETL processes. Practical experience in Azure, Azure Data Factory and Azure Databricks is preferred. Proven experience in Python PySpark and SQL SparkSQL programming skills. At least 3 years of experience in Information Technology – specifically in technical data roles e.g. data engineering, data analytics or data science. Knowledge of data management and storage solutions, including SQL Server Database, Azure Data Lake Storage or similar. Having good communication skills and being able to operate in a team environment. Excellent attention to detail and problem-solving skills related to data, data structures and algorithms. Key responsibilities will include: Contribute to the architecture and design of the Confluent Data Platform and ingestion patterns. Implement and support the scalable data solutions on the Microsoft Azure cloud platform, including Azure Databricks. Develop, maintain and optimise robust production data pipelines for data ingestion, processing, and transformation to support analytics, machine learning and other downstream data-driven services. + Ensure data security, quality and integrity across all of the pipelines and related data processes. Collaborate with the data product owner, analysts, data scientists, and business stakeholders to understand data requirements and implement effective data workflows. Assist in data initiatives and support technical teams including architects, analysts, software developers, and data scientists. Qualifications: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field. Certifications in Azure, Microsoft Fabric Data Engineering, Azure Databricks Data Engineering or similar or equivalent practical experience.

Title: Commercial Finance Business Analyst
Description: Company Description ABOUT AVERY DENNISON CORPORATION Avery Dennison (NYSE:AVY) is a global leader in labeling and packaging materials and solutions. The company’s applications and technologies are an integral part of products used in every major market and industry. With operations in more than 50 countries and more than 25,000 employees worldwide, Avery Dennison serves customers with insights and innovations that help make brands more inspiring and the world more intelligent. Headquarters in Glendale, California . Learn more at www.averydennison.com . Job Description ABOUT YOUR ROLE: Scope and general purpose of the role: Overall Commercial and FP&A support to Middle East Africa (MEA) Business and Region. Actively support the Commercial and Sales teams with commercial guidance. Custodian of BI development and related data integrity maintenance. YOUR RESPONSIBILITIES WILL INCLUDE, BUT NOT LIMITED TO: MONTH-END CLOSING: (10%) Submit weekly sales orders in HFM on time as required Update monthly rebates and discounts workings or perform review if prepared by others. Track sales discount agreements and arrange for credit note processing at designated contract time. Perform designated expense review (eg. samples) and raise appropriate accruals where expenses are missing or reallocate expenses where required. Submit regional flash reporting for Reflective and Tapes division and review final results for both Supplementary information preparation and submission to SSO. General support to the SSO during month end activities, in order to achieve HFM submission on day 3 Update sales information for Global customer scorecards FP & A SUPPORT: (20%) Update of various regionally assigned monthly and quarterly reporting schedules Prepare financial smartview templates for inclusion in the MBR presentation deck Assume responsibility for monthly regional reporting requirements including, but not limited to: + Flash report during financial closing week for responsible BU’s + Pulse report during 3rd week of the month for responsible BU’s + GM Scorecard (pricing updates) + Rolling forecast preparation + Preparation of first draft of the Business Review presentation deck and circulate to responsible persons for comments Perform commercial regional and local reporting as required Ensure team keeps to regional timelines in terms of monthly reporting Attend regional FP&A meetings calls as and when required Responsible for all regional reporting for the Reflective and Tapes businesses FORECAST, ANNUAL BUDGET AND STRATPLAN: (Ad-Hoc 10%) Assume accountability for monthly sales forecast file, and associated commercial trackers. Prepare schedules and reports required for annual budget pack Contribute towards preparation of presentation deck and reviews Preparation and upload of HFM data (Reflective and Tapes) Stratplan: Support with assigned responsibilities as part of Stratplan process, focussing on Industry and price related updates BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE: (15%) Manage Cognos (data warehouse) updates and uploads including data preparation and data reconciliation Update sales data required by the commercial team, (Eg. Sales Pivot file, DataStudio, etc) Develop reports as requested by internal customers Oversee and review PLT reporting and PLT Data Integrity Assume responsibility for all sales and commercial reporting Review master data integrity (BI driver) COMMERCIAL BUSINESS PARTNERING: (35%) Review and update price change request Perform workings and support for price change decisions Proactively review SKU profitability and discussions with Sales team Finance and Commercial related support to the Sales Teams Flag risk areas of low margins, pricing anomalies, etc Provide proactive commercial insights and feedback to Management Identify, interpret and advise on the impact of costs changes GENERAL: (10%) Provide support for stock take as and when required Support during statutory audit as and when required Training support to the business on areas of expertise i.e. Google, BI etc. General finance support to the business Responsible for VAT administration (return submissions, VAT recon, etc. Qualifications WHAT WE WILL BE LOOKING FOR: Be approachable to team members and have strong communication skills Computer literacy – Google suite, with Google Sheets and MS Excel at an Advance level BI experience – COGNOS capabilities Data warehousing and Integrated systems Commercial support understanding of key business drivers FP&A reporting Experience in regional reporting, prepare presentations and experience in a multinational environment COMPETENCIES REQUIRED: Building Partnerships Communication Contributing to Team Success Decision Making Energy Formal Presentation Information Monitoring Managing Work (includes Time Management) Planning and Organizing Stress Tolerance Technical Professional Knowledge and Skills Tenacity Work Standards Initiating Action Additional Information All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, gender identity, disability, protected veteran status or other protected status. EEOE M F Vet Disabled. All your information will be kept confidential according to EEO guidelines.

Title: Quality Analyst
Description: Please note, this is a non-technical role in a technical environment Purpose The Quality Analyst gathers, analyses, interprets data and reports on findings and insights for effective and efficient business management. Investigates new sources of data and ensures that data is of a good quality. The Quality Analyst has a wide toolset for data gathering, data manipulation, and proper interpretation. Responsibilities may include but not limited to: Interpret agent quality-monitoring results, recommended further action and monitor adherence. Incident Analysis (Categorisation, Root-Cause, Diagnostics, Resolution, Trends, Repeat Analysis, Highest call-loggers, etc.). Incident Analysis (Categorisation, Root-Cause, Diagnostics, Resolution). Trend Analysis (Highest Call-Loggers, Categories, Root Causes, Repeat Incidents). Support-level alignment between customer maintenance-lists and Call Logging tool. Be alert to deteriorating customer-service within the environment and intervene directly or by escalation. At specified intervals confirm and sign-off Stowe’s service-level conformance against contractual obligations. At specified intervals confirmand sign-off Stowe’s proposed Ad-hoc work for customer approval. Minimum Requirements: Senior Certificate 2 – year IT Diploma or equivalent Technical Service Desk Customer Service Numeracy Skills required Competencies: Must have strong analytical skills Must be well spoken Must be able to research to gain further knowledge Microsoft Office Suite, advanced Excel Skills Must have supervisory skills Should be able to work under limited supervision Advantageous Skills: Quality Assurance exposure Knowledge of Small Business Management and management reporting Fuel Industry Exposure and Knowledge Business Intelligence or Information System Qualification Point of Sale Retail Exposure Closing date: Friday, 21 September 2018 Job Type: Full-time Pay: From R9 000,00 per month Work Location: In person

Title: Supply Chain Analyst
Description: Purpose of the job | Be a first-class business partner for the James Sedgwick Whisky Distillery, Van Ryn distillery and JVs by generating and collating financial data, ensure integrity of financial data. Identify and access performance and risks and take or recommend business decisions and actions for the distillery and enable fact-based decision making and product and operating cost management. Be the guardian of HEINEKEN assets and reputation through improved financial controls, managed financial risks and clear & transparent information on business performance. Key responsibilities | Be a co-pilot to Sites and JVs Production teams in decision making through solicited and unsolicited advice based on financial analysis and in-depth knowledge of business operations. Support the business planning cycle of the Site (Strategic Plan, Annual Plan and Latest Estimates). Prepare and critically analyse monthly results and deliver timely management reports which include commentaries, KPIs, graphs, internal external insights and or benchmarks. Manage and assess business unit performance against standards including: OEE, O T, costing standards, Routing on SAP and recommend performance improvement or standards adjustment Analyse and report on weekly and monthly production order variances, provide early warnings on deviations, advise on corrective mitigating actions; review loss trends; ensure that the production Bill of Materials are correct and up to date. Ensure reliability & integrity of accounting records (accounts and cost centres) and financial information (P&L; and balance sheet) through regular controls and financial awareness of functions. Ensure short-term interval controls are effective. Continuously review sites and JVs financial control processes and identify possible risks. Propose and implement solutions controls to strengthen control environment and foster a culture of continuous improvement. Perform monthly audits to ensure adherence to internal control principles and assist in stock verification as and when required. Support business unit to ensure adherence to financial control policies (financial, IT and materials) and procedures and good corporate governance principles & practices. Adhere to Excise policies and procedures and ensure documentation and administration is suitably maintained. Ensure adherence and provide necessary support to the capital expenditure process as well as ensuring validity and accuracy of the asset register. Involved in stock management and assist with stock takes, identification of slow moving and obsolete stock. Escalate to management team any stock risks. Participate in cross site audits and stock takes. Develop level 2 & 3 management financial competencies capability in business unit. Implement new ideas and improve methods, systems and work processes that will lead to higher quality, better results, and reduced costs. Support and assist site managers in compiling OPEX and CAPEX budgets and forecasts. Co-ordinate the processing of budgets and forecasts Managing of the Monthly Barrel reconciliations across sites. Responsible for the accurate and timeous end to end processing and reconciliation of all financial transactions, management accounts and reporting relating to the JV’s. Job requirements + Education BCom Accounting or equivalent qualification completed. CIMA BCom Accounting honours advantages. + Experience Minimum of 3 years relevant experience in the area of Management Accounting and or Controlling, ideally in Supply Chain. FMCG experience advantageous. Intellectual curiosity and ability to search out facts without prior process guidance. Passion for delivering result, take ownership and translating insights into concise practical plans. Strong interpersonal communication influencing skills to challenge and win support and drive decision making. Excellent team player with strong oral and written communication skills. High level of cross functional cooperation: effectively partnering with various teams. Ability to work independently and under pressure. Drive for improvements and changes. Sound knowledge of MS office and SAP with specific importance to manufacturing. Ability to prepare simple medium complexity business cases and models.

Title: Data Management Analyst
Description: Position Overview: We are looking for an entry-level Data Management Analyst to join our team focused on our Investment Suite located in Cape Town, South Africa. This role will focus on and provide administrative support for clients’ use of MRI's in-house software applications. We are looking for a detail-oriented professional to collect data and documents from various sources and process monthly, quarterly, and annual financials as well as provide ongoing client training. You will maintain timely communication with clients, ensuring accurate and efficient data collection and processing of financial documents (Interims, Audits, Tax Returns, Budgets, Rent Rolls, etc.). You will be responsible for using in-house software to process data within designated SLAs, while delivering exceptional client service that exceeds expectations. Strong accountability and frequent collaboration with both clients and the internal team are essential. We operate a hybrid working model with 3 days of working from home per week. This role is based in the Cape Town Office, and the successful candidate will need to work partial US hours – 12:00 to 20:30 South African time, Monday to Friday. Responsibilities: Collect and organize data and documents from various sources Accurately process and review financial source documents, including Interims, Audits, Tax Returns, Budgets, and Rent Rolls Maintain timely, professional communication with clients and internal teams Take accountability for ensuring data is processed within required SLAs Use and maintain in-house data processing software Validate the collection and accuracy of financial documents Run client and internal training sessions when required Skills and Other Requirements: Bachelor’s Degree in Finance, Accounting, Real Estate, Business Administration or a related field 1 – 3 year’s experience in a similar role High attention to detail High level of accountability and collaboration Experience with Microsoft Excel Experience with MS SQL is an advantage Excellent written and verbal communication skills Knowledge of Finance and or Accounting Principles We’re obsessed with making this the best job you’ve ever had! We want our teams to love working here, so we’ve created some incredible perks for you to enjoy: We want our staff to love working here, and so we’ve created a few unique perks such as office breakfasts, quarterly lunches and virtual social events. Additionally, we value your input in your employee experience and have employee-led groups such as our DEI committee, employee resource groups such as Women and Allies, and our Pride Event Group Have confidence in your health with our offered Medical Aid Scheme. Invest in our competitive Personal Pension plan and help set you up for your future. Big on family? So are we! Here at MRI Software we recognise that your family is important, and being able to spend quality time with your family as it grows is a wonderful experience. Therefore, the MRI Software Parental Leave benefit is designed to give you the opportunity to spend time with your new arrival(s). Enjoy a fantastic work-life balance with 25 days of annual leave plus public Holidays, in addition to a bank of 16 hours of "Flex Time Off" to be used whenever and however you choose! Further your professional development with our Tuition Reimbursement Schemes Enjoy the flexibility of working from anywhere in the world for two weeks out of the year! MRI continues to strive to amaze as a global industry leader in real estate software. Whether you are joining as a new pride member or bringing your expertise back, your talent is important to maintaining MRI’s high client experience standard and continuing our growth in the PropTech space. Amazing growth takes amazing employees. Are you up to the challenge? As a global company, we believe diversity brings benefits for our people, customers and clients. This is why MRI Software is committed to being an inclusive employer, regardless of your ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, disability, age, military and veteran status. Diversity, Equality and Inclusion are values that are critical to our success; come and see for yourself. About Us From the day we opened our doors, MRI Software has built flexible, game-changing real estate software that powers thriving communities and helps make the world a better place to live, work and play. Fulfilling that mission is only possible because of one thing: exceptional people. People like you! Our people-first approach to PropTech is defining a new industry standard for client experiences that, quite frankly, can’t be duplicated. Experiences that deliver real value every day. And we know those experiences begin with our people. We believe MRI is more than just a workplace; it’s a connected community of people who truly feel they belong. Whether we’re investing in employee resource groups or providing tailored resources for each person to reach their full potential, we’re passionate about creating a work environment that makes you excited to show up every single day. At MRI, one of our core values is to strive to amaze. From the intelligent solutions we create to the culture we cultivate, that’s our goal every day. Because that’s what industry leaders do. Whether you’re joining as a new Pride member or rejoining us after a short time away, your talent is vital to us, our partners and our clients. Amazing growth requires amazing employees. Are you up to the challenge? We know confidence gap and imposter syndrome can get in the way of meeting remarkable candidates, so please don’t hesitate to apply. We’d love to hear from you! MRI is proud to be an inclusive employer. We welcome and celebrate diversity across all backgrounds, including ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, disability, age, military, veteran status and more. We believe that Belonging is a direct result of Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion. Those values are woven into the fabric of who we are and are foundational to our continued success. Title: Junior Credit Risk Analyst
Description: Weaver FinTech Group is a leading digital financial services ecosystem connecting consumers to smarter, more flexible ways to pay, borrow, and protect what matters most. Our brands — Pay Just Now and FinChoice — empower millions of South Africans through innovative Payments, Lending, and Insurance solutions. We’re a data-first organisation built on collaboration, innovation, and purpose: to make digital finance simpler, more inclusive, and more human. Junior Credit Risk Analyst PayJustNow is a Weaver Fintech company and South Africa's leading Buy Now, Pay Later platform, part of the same group as FinChoice. We let consumers split purchases into interest-free instalments at checkout across thousands of merchants, while giving retailers a powerful tool to grow sales. We are an innovative, consumer-facing fintech obsessed with customer experience, innovation and building great tech in pursuit of our vision to be our customers' favourite way to pay. Our team consists of passionate individuals that work in multi-disciplinary teams, where our strength in execution lies in our teamwork. Collectively we have driven significant customer growth and profitability, and we focus on the detail, being hands-on and executing against our plans – a scale-up that thinks and executes like a start-up. If you're looking for a place where you will positively impact lives, learn more about and contribute to the Fintech industry, and be involved in multiple products, projects and technologies, then look no further than PayJustNow. Role Overview As the Junior Credit Risk Analyst, you will help the business get more value out of every data source it touches. Working closely with senior analysts and the Credit Risk Executive, you will gather, clean and combine data from across the group, the credit bureaux and alternative data sources, and turn it into analysis that informs strategic credit risk decisions. This is a hands-on, learning-rich role for an early-career analyst who is curious about data and wants to see their analysis drive real decisions across underwriting, limits, collections and portfolio monitoring. More about the role Source, extract and prepare data from across the group, credit bureau feeds and alternative data sources, building clean, reliable datasets for analysis Help identify and unlock new value from existing and emerging data sources – spotting useful signals, new attributes and data quality issues Use SQL Python to obtain data from the Data Warehouse and support the maintenance of analytical data assets Perform 'what if' and ad-hoc analysis to support credit strategy across acquisition, limits, collections and recoveries Support the monitoring of risk-based strategies in acquisition to ensure only customers with the correct risk levels are acquired Help review credit limits to ensure the business exposure strategy is met Build and maintain reporting and dashboards that track credit quality of business booked and the credit portfolio Support the development and back-testing of scorecards and predictive models Use a data-oriented approach to work with others in solving business problems around profitability, marketing, risk and operations Assist the Credit Risk Executive and senior analysts on key projects, ensuring strong planning and communication throughout More about you Experience & Skills A minimum of 1–2 years' experience in Credit Risk, data analytics or a related field (recent graduates with strong analytical internships will be considered) A numerate degree e.g. BCom, B Bus Sci, B Eng, BSc Good computer literacy skills with proficiency in MS Excel The ability to creatively solve problems The ability to interrogate and interpret numerical and statistical information Advantages SQL & Snowflake Exposure to credit bureau and or alternative data sources Python R or equivalent Retail credit or BNPL experience Use of Gen AI Attributes & Behaviours Natural curiosity around data and problem solving Strong analytical skills and a self-motivated driver of performance Proactive and able to identify, highlight, analyse and action based on information available Able to work with other areas to ensure analytical outputs can be driven through to execution to create value Excellent interpersonal communication (verbal & written), assertiveness and judgment skills The ability to simply explain complex solutions Strong planning, organising and coordinating skills Exceptional attention to detail with a thorough approach to work A strong can-do attitude and an energetic positive approach A solid work ethic and a passion for digital Financial Services Eager to learn, coachable and entrepreneurial by nature More about us Reciprocity and trust are the foundation of how we work. We believe that each of us show up every day to do our best work and help one another and our customers. Strong, meaningful relationships underpin our culture and success, and we value authenticity and transparency. We value equity and strive to be fair and balanced. We celebrate our differences and understand how they contribute positively to the team. We offer a flexible working arrangement and hybrid working We support your professional development through coaching, training and bursaries We offer employee wellness and support through our ChoiceCare employee assistance programme We love celebrating your milestones with you

Title: Sales Analyst (6 Month Contract)
Description: Kenvue is currently recruiting for a: Sales Analyst (6 Month Contract) What we do At Kenvue, we realize the extraordinary power of everyday care. Built on over a century of heritage and rooted in science, we’re the house of iconic brands - including NEUTROGENA®, AVEENO®, TYLENOL®, LISTERINE®, JOHNSON’S® and BAND-AID® that you already know and love. Science is our passion; care is our talent. Who We Are Our global team is ~ 22,000 brilliant people with a workplace culture where every voice matters, and every contribution is appreciated. We are passionate about insights, innovation and committed to delivering the best products to our customers. With expertise and empathy, being a Kenvuer means having the power to impact millions of people every day. We put people first, care fiercely, earn trust with science and solve with courage – and have brilliant opportunities waiting for you! Join us in shaping our future–and yours. Role reports to: Senior Commercial Manager Location: Europe Middle East Africa, South Africa, Gauteng, Johannesburg Work Location: Fully Onsite What you will do Purpose Provide the Customer Development team with the required Commercial support, in particular with the Trade Promotional Management System, and the end to end life cycle of trade promotions, including budgeting, planning, payments deductions, and post-promotional analysis. Perform Ad-hoc Reporting when required to support customer engagements and business decision processes. Duties Commercial & Finance Support Ensures and maintains Promotional Events in the Trade Promotional Management System (TIPPS) for all approved agreements. Assist Customer Development team with event creation & invoice settlement, and ensure consistency in approach across business. Monitor and track accuracy of settlements of Promotional Events. Understand material master data fields influencing the order process. E.g. ex: Delivery plant material, Transportation zones, EDPAR and partner functions. Internal Reporting Ad hoc reporting and analysis duties as needed. Title: CRM & Data Analyst
Description: Job Title: CRM & Data Analyst Location: Cape Town - Sea Point Salary: R20,000 per month Working Hours: Rotational shifts, including two weekends per month Job Summary - We are seeking an experienced, detail-oriented, and analytical CRM & Data Analyst to join our fast-paced Financial Services Call Centre. The successful candidate will be responsible for maintaining accurate customer records, managing CRM data, generating daily operational reports, supporting lead management activities, and ensuring data integrity across multiple systems. This role requires strong administrative, reporting, and data management skills, with a high level of accuracy and attention to detail. Key Responsibilities - Capture, update, and maintain customer information within the CRM system.Ensure all client records are accurate, complete, and compliant with company standards and regulatory requirements. Process, verify, and update customer applications and supporting documentation. Conduct data cleansing, auditing, and quality assurance checks to maintain database integrity. Generate and distribute daily, weekly, and monthly operational and performance reports to management. Monitor lead volumes, campaign performance, agent productivity, and conversion metrics, highlighting trends and discrepancies. Maintain reporting trackers and ensure accurate record-keeping of all CRM and data-related activities. Manage lead allocation and customer database segmentation.Identify, investigate, and correct discrepancies within customer records and reporting data. Support call centre agents and management with customer information and data-related queries. Assist with data extraction, reporting requests, and operational analysis. Maintain confidentiality and security of customer information at all times. Meet daily, weekly, and monthly productivity, quality, and reporting targets Minimum Requirements - Matric (Grade 12) is essential. Minimum of 2 years' experience in a CRM, Data Administration, Data Capturing, Reporting. Previous experience within a Financial Services, Insurance, Lending, Banking, or Contact Centre environment is preferred. Strong computer literacy with proficiency in Microsoft Office, particularly Excel. Experience working with CRM systems and databases. Strong analytical, reporting, and problem-solving abilities. Excellent attention to detail and data accuracy. Strong verbal and written communication skills. Ability to work independently and within a team environment. Ability to work rotational shifts and two weekends per month. Competencies - High level of accuracy and attention to detail. Strong organisational and time-management skills. Excellent reporting and analytical capabilities. Ability to handle confidential information with discretion. Strong work ethic and ability to perform under pressure. Proactive, dependable, and solution-oriented mindset. Previous CRM, data administration, reporting, or financial call centre experience is essential. Pay: R20 000,00 per month Work Location: In person

Title: Realtime Analyst
Description: Company Description WNS, part of Capgemini, is an Agentic AI-powered leader in intelligent operations and transformation, serving more than 700 clients across 10 industries, including Banking and Financial Services, Healthcare, Insurance, Shipping and Logistics, and Travel and Hospitality. We bring together deep domain excellence - WNS’ core differentiator - with AI-powered platforms and analytics to help businesses innovate, scale, adapt and build resilience in a world defined by disruption.Our purpose is clear: to enable lasting business value by designing intelligent, human-led solutions that deliver sustainable outcomes and a differentiated impact. With three global headquarters across four continents, operations in 13 countries, 65 delivery centers and more than 66,000 employees, WNS combines scale, expertise and execution to create meaningful, measurable impact. Job Description Key Responsibilities 1. Real-Time Monitoring & Queue Management Monitor real-time queues across:Inbound Outbound voiceChat, Email, Back-office workstreams Track performance against key metrics:Service Level (SL)ASA, Queue depthAHT, Abandonment rate Ensure queues are balanced and staffed appropriately 2. Intraday Performance ManagementCompare actual performance vs forecast and schedule Identify variances and highlight risks proactively Execute immediate corrective actions to maintain SLAs:Skill reassignments Queue prioritizationTask redistribution 3. Adherence & Shrinkage Monitoring Track real-time schedule adherence at agent and team level Highlight non-adherence and coordinate with Operations for corrective action Monitor shrinkage (planned vs unplanned) and flag deviations 4. Real-Time Decision Making Recommend and execute intraday actions such as:Overtime (OT)Voluntary Time Off (VTO) Shift extensions or adjustments Manage contingencies during:Volume spikes System outages Staff shortages 5. Stakeholder Coordination Work closely with:Operations (Team Leaders Managers) Scheduling and Planning teams Act as a bridge between WFM and Operations for real-time updates Communicate performance insights, risks, and action plans clearly 6. Reporting & Dashboards Publish intraday reports capturing:Service level trends Queue performanceAdherence metrics Maintain RTA trackers and dashboards using:ExcelWFM tools Provide timely updates at defined intervals (hourly daily) 7. Tools & Systems Work on real-time monitoring using:Verint Genesys Aspect Ensure accuracy of real-time data feeds and dashboards Support automation and reporting enhancements 8. Governance & ComplianceEnsure adherence to:Client SLAs and KPIsoperational guidelines and governance Maintain logs of actions taken for audit and review purposes Qualifications Graduate in any discipline 2–4 years of experience in WFM (RTA Intraday management) Experience in BPO contact center environment is mandatory Exposure to multi-channel operations (voice & digital) preferred Additional Information US Rotational Night Shift

Title: Yield Analyst
Description: Le Boat, part of the Travelopia group , is the world’s leading provider of self-drive boating holidays, with over 55 years of experience. We offer an unforgettable holiday experience across Europe and Canada's inland waterways. Our fleet, crafted to suit all party sizes and budgets, requires no license or prior boating experience, making it accessible to everyone. With guests from around the globe, including North America, Germany, and France, Le Boat operates in 9 countries with France's Canal du Midi as the most famous and popular cruising ground. We are looking to hire a Yield Analyst to join our team on a 12-month fixed-term contract . Reporting to the Revenue Management Director, you will support pricing decisions across our fleet and destinations. You will analyse sales, booking pace, utilisation, and competitor pricing, recommending and implementing pricing, discounting, and product actions to help achieve revenue and occupancy targets. Pricing is primarily managed in Excel, supported by Power BI dashboards and Neptune (our reservations system). Over the next 12 months, we are expanding the use of AI within our revenue management workflow, using tools such as Claude, Copilot, and Python-based models to automate analysis, improve forecasting, and identify pricing opportunities more quickly. You will play a key role in this transition and act as the go-to person for pricing-related queries across Commercial, Marketing, Fleet, and MI. What we’ll offer: Competitive base salary of R300,000-400,000 per annum Flexible hybrid working (2 days per week in our Cape Town office) Generous paid time off, plus a day off to celebrate your birthday Group-wide travel discounts A small, high-impact team where your work is visible to commercial leadership from day one What you’ll do: Revenue Management Analyse sales, booking pace, utilisation and forecasts, implementing pricing and promotional actions to achieve revenue targets. Monitor competitor pricing across key source markets and maintain pricing and discount structures in Neptune and Excel. Evaluate performance by boat type, base and source market, recommending corrective actions where required. Assess the effectiveness of promotions and discounting activity on revenue and margin. Provide pricing support to Marketing and respond to internal commercial queries. Forecasting & Analysis Build and maintain demand forecasts using booking pace, historical trends and booking curves. Identify emerging performance trends and recommend actions to improve revenue outcomes. Support pricing tests and analysis, including elasticity and promotional effectiveness. AI & Tooling Use AI tools (e.g. Claude, Copilot, ChatGPT) to support analysis, forecasting, reporting and communication. Help identify, test and implement AI-driven improvements to pricing, forecasting and reporting processes. Support the development and rollout of new pricing, forecasting and automation tools. Reporting & Insight Build and maintain reporting in Power BI to track pricing performance, occupancy and margin in near real time. Produce a clear weekly pricing pack for the Revenue Management Director and commercial leadership. Provide cover for the MI team’s weekly reporting suite during absence. Systems Become the expert user of Neptune (reservations system), understanding how pricing rules, durations and availability are configured and identifying improvement opportunities. Work with the inventory team on fleet planning inputs, including base availability, maintenance windows, route disruptions and duration rules. What you’ll bring: Essential Strong numerical and analytical skills with exceptional attention to detail. Advanced Excel skills — comfortable with complex formulas, pivot tables, Power Query, and large datasets. Excel is the primary tool for this role. Experience building reports and dashboards in Power BI. Confident using AI tools to support analysis, reporting and problem-solving. Commercially minded, with the ability to balance margin, demand, and customer impact. Strong communication skills with the ability to explain pricing decisions to stakeholders. Calm under pressure and comfortable working in a fast-paced, highly seasonal environment. Inquisitive and detail-oriented, with a willingness to challenge assumptions and question data. Proactive and able to work independently with minimal direction. Desirable Previous experience in pricing, yield or revenue management, ideally in travel, hospitality, airlines or car rental. Experience applying AI tools to forecasting, pricing, or revenue problems (beyond basic tool usage). Understanding of prompt engineering and how to structure AI inputs for reliable analytical output. Python and or SQL skills for data extraction, manipulation or automation. Exposure to demand forecasting, price elasticity or segmentation analysis. Familiarity with reservation systems, PMS or revenue management platforms. Experience with A B testing or commercial experimentation frameworks. We believe people perform best when they can be their true selves, and diverse teams drive better results. We’re committed to fostering a diverse, equitable, and inclusive environment where everyone can succeed. Travelopia ensures an inclusive workplace for all. If you need accessibility support during the recruitment process, please inform us here: Talent@Travelopia.com LI-NO1 LI-HYBRID

Title: Senior Retail Media Insights Analyst
Description: Senior Retail Media Insights Analyst Role Purpose The Insights function is enabled by large-scale enterprise data assets, advanced analytics, and applied research capabilities to generate actionable insights that support commercial decision-making, customer growth, and business performance. The Senior Retail Media Insights Analyst is responsible for analysing complex datasets, identifying trends and opportunities, and translating findings into clear, actionable insights that inform planning, optimisation, and performance improvement across the business. The role partners closely with internal teams and stakeholders to ensure data-led insights are understood, applied correctly, and embedded into decision-making processes. This role requires strong analytical capability, the ability to connect data to business context, and the discipline to deliver accurate, high-quality insight outputs at scale. Job Description Data Analysis and Insight Generation Collect, clean, analyse and interpret data from defined internal and external sources to generate actionable insights. Identify trends, patterns, risks and opportunities through structured analysis and deep dives. Perform root cause analysis to address defined business questions or performance challenges. Ensure data accuracy, integrity and consistency across all analyses and insight outputs. Insight Translation and Storytelling Translate analytical findings into business-relevant insight narratives and recommendations. Present insights in clear, concise and stakeholder-appropriate language. Create insight packs, presentations and reporting outputs aligned to business objectives. Reporting and Performance Measurement Develop standard and ad hoc reports, dashboards and commentary to track performance. Apply appropriate metrics, benchmarks and performance indicators to support decision-making. Support the continuous improvement of reporting frameworks and measurement approaches. Stakeholder Collaboration Partner with cross-functional teams to understand insight requirements and priorities. Act as a trusted analytics partner by supporting effective use of insights in planning and optimisation. Support insight enablement by ensuring outputs are correctly interpreted and applied. Continuous Improvement and Learning Stay current with industry trends, data methodologies and analytical techniques. Contribute to improved analytical practices, tools and processes within the Insights function. Role-Specific Responsibilities: Senior Retail Media Analyst Analyse retail media performance data across campaigns, platforms and channels. Generate campaign-level and post-campaign insights to inform optimisation, planning and future media strategies. Support media performance measurement frameworks, tracking methodologies and tagging standards. Translate retail media data into actionable insights for sales teams, media partners and clients. Contribute to media planning inputs by aligning insights to audience, category and campaign objectives. Stay current with retail media trends, tools and measurement methodologies. Qualifications and Experience Degree or Diploma in Business, Marketing, Analytics, or a related field – (essential). +5 years' experience in a similar capacity, buying or category management – (essential). Demonstratable hands-on analytics experience - generating actionable insights and doing data deep-dives - (essential). Experience translating data into digestible insights for media sales and client campaign recommendations and presentations - (essential) Proficiency in MS Office and CRM software (e.g., Salesforce) - (essential) Media analysis, data analytics, or related roles experience in the advertising or marketing industry- (desirable). Demonstratable ability influencing, establishing and maintaining strong relationships with management, internal partners, and external clients - (desirable) Key Competencies and Work Ethic Retail Media Insights and Reporting – Sound understanding of retail media processes for data collection, processing, analysis to provide accurate reports and or dashboards that meet the requirements of the target audience. Applies sound knowledge and skills in using data visualization tools, developing dashboards and selecting the most relevant key performance metrics in a manner that will enable insights and decision-making. Analytical thinker and resourcefulness- Readily comprehend new and relatively complex concepts and information, investigates courses of action to identify the most appropriate solution or develop alternative solutions. Able to analyze retail media insights with a desire to learn more about the trends shaping consumer behavior today, and in the future. Customer focused - obsessed about the customer and delivering a digital enriched customer journey experience. Collaborative partnering - Builds meaningful and sound relationships with suppliers across brands, consumer insights, ecommerce, and advanced analytics. Open, honest and direct and comfortable giving and receiving constructive feedback. Thinks and acts both independently as well as collaboratively. Communication skills - Speaks clearly, audibly and at an appropriate pace and provides credible points of an argument in own area of expertise. Writes clearly and succinctly, using correct spelling and grammar; and provides simple points in a straightforward and factual manner. Strong command of the English language with excellent presentation and story telling skills- Ability to articulates ideas and concepts in a creative and meaningful way. Detailed, organized and quality focused – Has an affinity for detail, structure, and efficiency. Is diligent and vigilantly watches over work processes, tasks, and outputs to ensure accuracy while independently actioning and correcting any quality concerns. Critical thinker and independent problem solver. Time management, planning and operational efficiency - able to prioritize a high volume of tasks simultaneously in a fast-paced unpredictable environment. Someone who can spread themselves across multiple tasks simultaneously by working smartly, efficiently, and effectively. Organized with strong time, delivery, and planning management. Adapting and responding to change - adjusts to a fast paced, changing environment; tolerates ambiguity; adapts interpersonal style to suit different people or situations. Staying up to date with global media trends and insights. Title: Digital Data Analyst (Rainmaker Media)
Description: Value Proposition Powered by Africa's largest retailer, Rainmaker Media is positioned to reach over 80% of SA's shoppers directly through its digital and physical media channels. We have exclusive access to the continent's largest retail audience and are using rich shopper data and advanced analytics to create the continent's first precision retail media service. We're currently building a cutting edge, collaborative, passionate team and seeking the best talent there is to join us in shaping our new culture and delivery ambitions! Role Purpose To streamline data acquisition through advanced tagging and Mobile Measurement Partner (MMP) management, delivering automated, AI-enhanced ecommerce insights in collaboration with the broader ShopriteX Data teams. Role Description 1. Digital Data Collection & Tagging Infrastructure Own the end-to-end implementation and management of digital tracking via Google Tag Manager (GTM) and other relevant tagging technologies. MMP Management: Maintain and optimise the AppsFlyer setup to ensure accurate mobile app attribution and data collection integrity across all mobile campaigns. Ensure precise data capture across all digital touchpoints to support attribution, conversion tracking, and audience segmentation.
