Title: Senior Frontend Engineer, Accounting - 100% Remote - EMEA
Description: NOTE: This is a FULLY remote role, but the candidate must be within EMEA to collaborate with their team, peers, and internal customers. You do not have to be in the specific country or city shown in this listing, but please only apply if you are physically based within EMEA. Trusted by 20,000+ property managers worldwide, Hostaway is an industry leading, AI-powered vacation rental management platform designed for professional short-term rental operators. Hostaway brings everything together for greater efficiency and faster growth. Profitable, high-growth, and the first short-term rental PMS unicorn . Join us at our most exciting stage yet! Learn more about our recent valuation and story here. About the role We’re looking for a delivery-focused Senior Frontend Engineer to help build Hostaway’s Trust Accounting white-label product . This role will have a direct impact on the financial operations of Hostaway’s customers by helping them keep compliant records, manage funds belonging to property owners, and deliver accurate, reliable financial workflows. This is a greenfield opportunity on a new team responsible for designing and delivering a financial subsystem that property managers can trust: accurate, compliant, scalable, and auditable. You will play a key role in shaping the frontend architecture, user experience, delivery practices, and engineering standards from day one. You’ll work closely with Product, Design, Backend Engineering, Finance, and Legal to build intuitive and robust interfaces for trust accounting, payments-related workflows, ledger visibility, reconciliation, reporting, and operational controls. What you’ll do: Develop and build scalable, high-quality frontend components and features for Hostaway’s Trust Accounting white-label product. Implement intuitive, performant, and reliable user workflows for financial operations, including trust accounting, payment visibility, fund movement tracking, reconciliation, and reporting. Build and evolve a flexible, white-label React application that can support multiple customer configurations and branded experiences. Contribute to the frontend architecture of a secure, scalable, auditable, and compliant financial subsystem. Develop and maintain reusable component libraries and contribute to the evolution of the design system. Ensure frontend performance optimization, responsive layouts, accessibility, and cross-browser compatibility. Document frontend architecture, components, patterns, and key technical decisions for knowledge sharing and long-term maintainability. Proactively identify and drive improvements in frontend systems, delivery reliability, observability, and user experience. 6+ years of experience in SaaS software development, with strong frontend engineering experience. Strong proficiency in React, TypeScript, JavaScript, CSS, and HTML . Proven experience building scalable, maintainable frontend applications in product-focused engineering environments. Experience working with accounting, ledger, payment, or financial operations products is a strong plus. Experience with state management solutions such as Redux, Redux-saga, Context APIs, or similar patterns. Experience building white-label or highly configurable frontend applications is strongly preferred. Strong understanding of accessibility, frontend performance optimization, responsive design, and cross-browser compatibility. Experience with testing frameworks such as Jest, Cypress, Playwright, or similar. Experience working in agile environments with distributed, cross-functional teams and rapidly shifting priorities. Nice to Have Experience with trust accounting, escrow, client money handling, property management accounting, or owner statement workflows. Experience with embedded payments, payment processors, payment operations, or fintech products. Experience building dashboards or reporting interfaces for finance, accounting, or operations teams. Experience contributing to design systems or reusable component libraries. Ability to design interfaces that support auditability, traceability, permissions, and operational review for financial workflows. Familiarity with compliance-sensitive product areas, especially where accuracy, audit trails, and customer trust are essential. What success looks like: The trust accounting whitelabel is delivered reliably, accurately, trusted by customers and regulators, and integrated seamlessly into the Hostaway platform. The product is high throughput, low error, and provides accurate data to customers. Compliance and financial correctness are vital. Cross-functional collaboration with Finance, Legal, Product, and Engineering runs smoothly. What we offer Competitive Compensation: We offer competitive pay based on market rates in the country of the applicant. 100% Remote: Enjoy the freedom to work from anywhere within your country of residence—be it a co-working space, your home office, or even your dining room table. The choice is yours. Just don’t ask to work in our office (we don’t have one). Equity: Every role in our company comes with valuable stock options in a fast-growing and profitable company. This ensures we all share in the company’s success. Values-Driven Leadership: Our Core Values are not just words we’ve written to make us feel good. We leverage them daily when making strategic and tactical decisions. Professional Growth: Our rapid growth offers unparalleled learning and development opportunities, along with a multitude of career advancement opportunities. Annual Paid Leave: The specific amounts vary by country and are aligned with country and or contract-specific norms. Geographic Specific Benefits: As an international employer, we offer different country-specific benefits such as Health Insurance and Pensions in countries where these perks are customary. The specifics depend on the country of the applicant. Dynamic Team Culture: As a global company with team members in over 40 countries, our diverse and international culture fuels our innovation and creativity, providing a key pillar to our success (and making it a lot of fun to work here). Thank you for your interest. If you apply for this role, you will receive an email from our Talent Acquisition team after your application has been reviewed alongside the qualifications for this role and the qualifications of others who have applied.

Title: Azure / AWS Cloud Engineer for Data Science
Description: DVT is one of the top software development companies on the continent. Our engineers consult on cutting-edge platforms at leading enterprises in South Africa and globally, with a strong culture of learning and technical excellence across the Azure and AWS ecosystems. We are building a pipeline of Cloud Engineers focused on Data Science enablement to support Standard Bank (SBSA) and related data-platform engagements. This is a client-facing consulting role combining data engineering, DevOps automation and platform run support on the Microsoft Azure stack (AWS exposure beneficial). You will provision and operate secure, well-governed data platforms, build and maintain SQL SSIS data pipelines, and keep production systems healthy under the bank's incident, change and release processes. The ideal candidate is a hands-on problem solver who is equally comfortable engineering data pipelines, automating cloud infrastructure, and providing disciplined production support in a regulated financial-services environment. DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES Cloud Platform Engineering & Administration (Azure, or AWS equivalent) Provision and manage Azure resources — virtual machines, storage, networking and integration runtimes. Maintain the availability, performance and security of the data platform. Monitor infrastructure health across compute, storage, networking and access. Manage patching, upgrades and ongoing platform maintenance. Support hybrid setups, including on-premises to cloud connectivity where applicable. Data Engineering Optional: Develop and maintain MS SQL database objects — stored procedures, T-SQL, tables, views and table types. Optional: Build, schedule and maintain ETL data pipelines using SSIS (and Azure Data Factory integration runtimes). Optional: Integrate to the data platform across Silver and Gold layers; ensure data quality, lineage and reliability. Databricks workloads where required (optional advantageous — not mandatory). Maintain documentation for assumptions, logic and version control. DevOps & Automation Build and maintain CI CD pipelines (AWS Code Pipeline, Azure DevOps, GitHub Actions or similar) for application and infrastructure delivery. Apply version control discipline using Git across all code, pipelines and configuration. Automate provisioning and configuration using Python and Infrastructure-as-Code (Terraform ARM Bicep; CloudFormation CDK beneficial for AWS). Establish reusable, well-governed platform build patterns. Identity, Security & Compliance Manage Identity & Access — RBAC, managed identities and Key Vault integration. Ensure platform compliance: security, encryption and audit requirements. Operate license-to-operate controls including DR, backup and security controls. Work with infrastructure and security teams on incidents and escalations. Monitoring, Support & Run-the-Org Monitor logs and alerts using Azure Monitor and Log Analytics; configure proactive alerting. Provide production (Run-the-Org) support: troubleshooting, bug fixes, monitoring and corrective maintenance per the bank's Incident and Problem Management process. Test, document and implement changes per the bank's Change and Release Management process. Participate in disaster-recovery testing, planning and documentation; adhere to and mitigate audit compliance findings. Engineering Delivery (Agile SAFe ) Evaluate user requirements; advise on design, content and architectural alignment of engineered features. Provide effort estimation and sequencing; develop, configure and optimise features. Perform component and assist with system integration testing; plan and execute cutover migration activities. Deploy to production, provide quality assurance on new solutions, and transfer skills to bank teams. Participate in SAFe Agile ceremonies (PI Planning, stand-ups, Scrum of Scrums, System Solution Demos, Inspect & Adapt) and own allocated story items. Required Experience and Skills 5+ years in Cloud Engineering, DevOps, Data Engineering or related roles, with hands-on delivery in regulated enterprise environments. Mandatory: MS SQL (including stored procedures), SSIS, Microsoft Azure cloud, and version control (Git). Strong Python for automation and data tooling (highly advantageous). Azure platform operations: VMs, storage, networking, integration runtimes, Azure Monitor, Log Analytics, RBAC, managed identities, Key Vault. AWS platform operations: EC2, storage, Route-53, Cloudwatch CI CD pipeline construction and Infrastructure-as-Code (Terraform ARM Bicep). Production Run-the-Bank support: incident, problem, change and release management; DR and backup. Experience in banking or financial-services regulated environments. Beneficial (not required): AWS engineering (EC2, ECS EKS, Lambda, S3, IAM, CloudFormation CDK), Databricks, Azure Data Factory Synapse, Power BI. Minimum Requirements Matric (Grade 12) certificate Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Engineering or a related field (or equivalent practical experience). At least two (foundation and solution architect associate) one relevant professional cloud certification (Microsoft Azure or AWS; Kubernetes equivalent acceptable). South African work authorisation or valid work permit Who we are:

Title: Logistics Specialist (AWS Data Center), Data Center Communities
Description: DESCRIPTION Be a part of operating the world's largest cloud computing infrastructure. Amazon Web Services is seeking a motivated, hardworking individual for the Data Center Logistics Specialist position. The Logistics Specialist reviews data center inventory, inbound shipments, and ongoing demand to ensure that proper stock levels are maintained to support build and repair activities. Working with the Logistics Manager, they create plans to optimise the flow of parts through their lifecycle — tracking inventory location, movement, age, and turns. They work closely with Data Center Operations Technicians and Engineers to fulfil parts quickly and accurately. They work with Procurement and Hardware Engineering to source new parts and interface directly with vendors to process RMAs and receive replacements. They are responsible for accurate data entry and resolving questions from peer organisations. The ideal Logistics Specialist will dive deep into data to understand the data center asset lifecycle. They maintain up-to-date metrics dashboards and create reports for internal and external suppliers and management as required. AWS Infrastructure Services owns the design, planning, delivery, and operation of all AWS global infrastructure. In other words, we're the people who keep the cloud running. We support all AWS data centers and all of the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that ensure our customers have continual access to the innovation they rely on. You'll join a diverse team of engineers, logistics specialists, security experts, and operations managers. You'll experience an inclusive culture that welcomes bold ideas and empowers you to own them to completion Key job responsibilities Coordinate inbound and outbound deliveries, working with vendors, carriers, and transportation teams Receive and ship parts — both physically and in inventory systems — ensuring records are accurate at all times Work closely with engineering and operations teams to fulfill parts requests quickly and accurately Resolve inventory queries and maintain accurate data entry across systems Execute stock transfers between locations locally and globally to optimise parts availability Manage inventory through regular cycle counts, storage organisation, cleanliness, and safety of all workspaces Process warranty returns (RMAs) directly with vendors and receive replacement parts Operate pallet jacks, trolleys, and carts to move materials throughout the facility Support ad hoc analysis and projects based on business needs This is a physical role in a warehouse-style environment. You'll be on your feet for extended periods, lifting and carrying materials (up to 18kg), and working in conditions that may include varying temperatures and noise levels. Logistics Specialists adhere to strict safety standards, maintain high quality, and may travel to support other locations within and outside the country. A day in the life Your day starts with safety — checking your area is hazard-free and secure. From there, you're at the loading dock receiving shipments, scanning parts into systems, and keeping stock levels right. You'll run cycle counts, process hardware returns, and keep workspaces organised. Beyond the routine, you'll use AI tools and the latest tech to find better ways of working. You'll collaborate globally, share ideas, and contribute to projects that improve operations across regions. It's physical, fast-paced, and keeps the cloud running. About the team Our logistics team supports a growing data center cluster, ensuring critical infrastructure components are always available to keep AWS services running for customers. We're a tight-knit team that values safety, accuracy, and innovation. We encourage our team members to find better ways of working — whether through new tools, automation, or process improvements — and to collaborate with logistics peers globally. Diverse Experiences AWS values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn't followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don't let it stop you from applying. Why AWS? Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world's most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that's why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses. Inclusive Team Culture AWS values curiosity and connection. Our employee-led and company-sponsored affinity groups promote inclusion and empower our people to take pride in what makes us unique. Our inclusion events foster stronger, more collaborative teams. Our continual innovation is fueled by the bold ideas, fresh perspectives, and passionate voices our teams bring to everything we do. Mentorship & Career Growth We're continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth's Best Employer. That's why you'll find endless knowledge-sharing, mentorship and other career-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better-rounded professional. Work Life Balance We value work-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why flexible work hours and arrangements are part of our culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there's nothing we can't achieve in the cloud. BASIC QUALIFICATIONS Experience as a logistics professional supporting inventory cycle counts, shipments, maintenance, stock control functions and reverse logistics PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS Experience using computers and Microsoft Office (Outlook, Word, Excel) Amazon is an equal opportunities employer, and we value your passion to discover, invent, simplify and build. We welcome applications from all members of society irrespective of age, sex, disability, sexual orientation, race, religion or belief. Amazon is strongly committed to diversity within its community and especially welcomes applications from South African citizens who are members of designated groups who may contribute to Employment Equity within the workplace and the further diversification of ideas. In this regard, the relevant laws and principles associated with Employment Equity will be considered when appointing potential candidates. We are required by law to verify your ability to work lawfully in South Africa. Amazon requires that you submit a copy of either your identity document or your passport and any applicable work permit if you are a foreign national, along with an updated curriculum vitae. Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https: amazon.jobs content en how-we-hire accommodations for more information. If the country region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.

Description: About Us Lancia Insights helps businesses make better decisions by consolidating complex business data into meaningful, decision-ready insights. We work with senior leadership teams, CFO's, sales teams, and private equity stakeholders, primarily in the UK and Europe. Our work focuses on creating custom analytics platforms for our clients and spans data consolidation, dashboarding, data warehousing, automation, and AI-enabled business solutions. We operate as a highly collaborative and hands-on team. We are curious problem-solvers who enjoy figuring things out, learning quickly, and building practical solutions that create measurable value for clients. The Role We are looking for a commercially minded and highly curious Analyst to join our growing team. This role sits at the intersection of client engagement, business problem solving, and AI-enabled delivery. You will work directly with clients to understand their problems, then use modern data and AI tooling to build and deliver the solutions yourself, from first conversation through to a working product in the client's hands. A technical background is not a prerequisite. We provide the stack, the tooling, and the delivery patterns. What we are hiring for is a particular way of thinking: the ability to decompose ambiguous business problems from first principles, to move fluently between commercial language and solution design, and an instinct to build rather than only specify. This is not a traditional analyst or product role. We are looking for someone who is energised by working in an AI-forward environment, takes ownership, learns aggressively, and wants to help shape both client outcomes and the future direction of the business. Key Responsibilities Build and maintain strong relationships with clients and key stakeholders Lead regular client check-ins and strategic discussions Engage confidently with CFOs, senior management teams, sales leaders, and shareholders Understand client business models, operational challenges, and strategic priorities Translate business requirements into solution designs you will help build and deliver Build, prototype, and deliver client solutions hands-on using modern data and AI tooling Identify opportunities to improve reporting, analytics, workflows, and strategic visibility and iterate on solutions live with clients Assist in shaping and evolving the company’s product and service offering Participate in strategic business and commercial discussions with clients Understand key commercial drivers and business performance metrics Use modern data platforms, AI tools, and business technologies as your daily toolkit Learn internal systems, workflows, and solution frameworks Requirements Essential: 3–5 years of experience in a field where you have had to decompose business problems and deliver outcomes e.g. strategic consulting, finance, business analysis or engineering Strong business and commercial acumen Excellent communication and interpersonal skills Confidence engaging with senior stakeholders and executives Strong first-principles problem-solving, able to decompose ambiguous business problems from scratch Highly self-motivated with a proactive mindset Comfortable operating in a fast-paced and unstructured environment Builder's instinct: you reach for tools and prototype your way to an answer rather than waiting for a spec, and you are eager to do this in an AI-forward environment Advantageous: Experience working with reporting or business intelligence solutions Exposure to data analytics, dashboards, or reporting platforms Experience in consulting or client-facing environments Exposure to modern data stack tooling (e.g. SQL, Python, warehousing) or AI tooling advantageous but not required; we will teach the stack Working Environment We are a small, highly collaborative in-office team based in Somerset West. This role is best suited to someone who enjoys working closely with others, thrives in entrepreneurial environments, prefers action over bureaucracy, and wants meaningful exposure to both business strategy and technology. Why Join Us Direct exposure to senior decision-makers and strategic business discussions Build at the intersection of AI, data, and business strategy — with the tools, autonomy, and client exposure to do it properly High-ownership, high-velocity learning environment, AI-forward by default Significant growth potential within a rapidly evolving business Exposure to international clients and business environments Opportunity to help shape the future direction of the company

Title: Data & Operations Analyst
Description: At Incanda, data drives our decisions, supports our operations, and enables our continued growth. As a Business Systems and Data Analyst, you will play a critical role in managing and optimizing our business systems, maintaining data integrity across platforms, and delivering meaningful insights that support operational and strategic decision-making. We are looking for an analytical and detail-oriented professional who enjoys working with data, improving processes, and collaborating with stakeholders across the business. Manage business systems, data integrity, and reporting processes. Key Responsibilities: Maintain and manage data feeds between Paradigm, Azure databases, and other business systems Develop, maintain, and enhance Business Intelligence (BI) dashboards and reporting tools Analyse operational and production data to provide actionable business insights Create, maintain, and ensure the accuracy of inventory codes and Bills of Materials (BOMs) Support costing accuracy through BOM maintenance and variance analysis Coordinate new product setup, code creation, and system integration requirements Monitor and manage order processing workflows and resolve system-related issues Liaise with internal stakeholders and external partners to ensure effective data and system management Skills and Requirements: Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities Excellent attention to detail and commitment to data accuracy Effective communication and stakeholder management skills Ability to manage multiple processes and priorities simultaneously Proficiency in database management and Business Intelligence tools Strong understanding of business systems and operational processes Minimum Requirements: Grade 12 (Matric) Bachelor's degree in Information Systems, Data Analytics, Industrial Engineering, Computer Science, or a related field 2–3 years' experience in Computer Programming, Data Analysis, or a similar role Experience working with SQL databases Knowledge of MRP and Business Operating Systems Experience with data reporting and dashboard development Turn data into insights. Title: Senior/Lead Technical Architect
Description: About the company Utopia is an ultra-luxury hospitality and lifestyle brand creating a global ecosystem of properties and experiences. We develop private villa retreats in beautiful locations around the world, each with high-end service. Utopia also offers a wider ecosystem of leisure experiences and services available both to those staying on property and to external visitors. Utopia designs and builds its properties in-house. Our team works across the full journey: from concept and architecture to construction, interiors, landscape, guest experience, and long-term operation. For architects and designers, this means working on properties from the ground up, with the ability to influence how each place is planned, built, and experienced. For hospitality professionals, it means joining a brand where service is personal, attentive, and built around each guest’s stay. We’re a young, ambitious team with high standards and a hands-on approach. We value strong ideas, practical thinking, attention to detail, and people who are ready to take responsibility for their part of the work. If you’re interested in architecture, design, hospitality, or guest experience, we invite you to grow with Utopia. About the role We're looking for a technically rigorous Senior Lead Technical Architect to translate design intent into fully coordinated, precise, and buildable documentation across our boutique hospitality projects. You will serve as the key technical authority within the design team — bridging concept and construction across RIBA Stages 3–5, while collaborating closely with design, engineering, and procurement. This role exists to ensure that quality, constructability, and coordination are preserved from design development through delivery. Location & Collaboration Format This is a fully remote collaboration. We collaborate in Сentral European UK timezones. Utopia does not provide visa sponsorship or a relocation package for this position. Responsibilities: Translate concept and schematic designs into coordinated, buildable Revit models and full documentation packages across RIBA Stages 3–5. Develop precise technical layouts, assemblies, and construction-level details in Revit, ensuring alignment with design intent, constructability, and regulatory requirements. Coordinate architectural models with structural, MEP, and interior design inputs using BIM workflows and Navisworks; resolve and prevent cross-discipline clashes. Lead the development of project-specific façade, roof, and technical typologies grounded in construction logic and material performance. Own quality control across Revit models and documentation; conduct technical reviews and flag risks proactively. Build and manage project families, typologies, and detail libraries to support efficient design development and team-wide standardization. Support tender and construction documentation, ensuring full alignment with specifications, codes, and local regulations. Collaborate with the procurement team on technical inputs related to material quantities, specifications, and product compatibility. Supervise and guide BIM modelers and junior technical staff; contribute to building department-wide technical workflows and standards. Requirements: Bachelor's or Master's degree in Architecture. 5–10 years of experience in architectural design, with a strong focus on technical development and construction documentation. Demonstrated experience on low-rise residential and hospitality projects — private homes, villas, hotels, or resorts. Expert proficiency in Revit, including parametric families, freeform modeling, and terrain modeling. Hands-on experience with Navisworks for BIM coordination and clash detection. Solid understanding of construction methodologies, material performance, and multi-disciplinary integration. Fluent English, written and verbal. Portfolio required. Nice to Have: Proficiency in Rhino, AutoCAD, or Enscape. Experience contributing to early-stage feasibility or RIBA Stage 1–2 design inputs. Familiarity with BIM automation tools and emerging technical design workflows. We Offer: Global project exposure — collaborate on luxury hospitality projects across Europe, Africa, and Latin America. A truly international team — collaborate across cultures, disciplines, and time zones with people bringing diverse perspectives and expertise. High ownership environment — we value initiative, independent thinking, and people who are ready to take responsibility for meaningful work. Growth opportunities — as we scale rapidly, we create new opportunities internally and support team members in expanding their scope and responsibilities. Flexible formats of collaboration — depending on the role, we offer fully remote, hybrid, and on-site opportunities designed to support effective collaboration and individual productivity. Work on projects from concept to realization — contribute across the full architectural journey, from early vision and planning to execution and operational experience. Cross-disciplinary collaboration — collaborate closely with interiors, landscape, construction, operations, and guest experience teams from the earliest stages. High design standards — join a team deeply focused on detail, materiality, atmosphere, and timeless hospitality design. By submitting this application, I agree that my personal data will be collected, processed, and retained by the company solely for the purposes of managing and assessing my candidacy. Notice: This is a remote position based in South Africa.

Title: Senior Salesforce Data Cloud Specialist
Description: Are you an architectural expert ready to build the "Single Version of Truth" for one of South Africa's largest retailers? PEP is seeking a highly skilled Senior Salesforce Data Cloud Specialist to join our team and become the custodian of our customer data revolution.This role is pivotal in strategically ingesting, harmonizing, and activating millions of customer data points to enable real-time, data-driven customer engagement. You will ensure our data architecture is high-performing and cost-effective, directly managing Salesforce Consumption Model efficiency and maximizing ROI on Flex Credits Key Responsibilities Strategic Data Architecture & Ingestion: Architect "Activation-Ready" data flows from POS, Fintech, and Loyalty systems, while ensuring a high data velocity. Design and maintain the Data Model Object (DMO) mapping strategy and relational data models that underpin unified customer profiles. Technical Segmentation & Insight Enablement: Build high-performance segments and deploy Data Science propensity and churn models into actionable Data Cloud segments, reducing time-to-market for complex audience creation. Surface segmentation capabilities and data insights to marketing stakeholders in accessible, non-technical formats Data Integrity & Compliance: Act as the final authority on data accuracy, ensuring 100% variance reconciliation and zero incidents of POPIA non-compliance, including successful automation of "Right to be Forgotten" requests. Implement and maintain consent management and suppression data flows that feed correctly into Marketing Cloud exclusion logic Operational Automation: Drive real-time business actions with a >95% success rate of Data Actions triggering journeys in Marketing Cloud. Reporting, Analytics & Stakeholder Enablement:Build and maintain data pipelines that feed campaign performance dashboards in Marketing Intelligence (new SKU previously known as MCI) on Salesforce core platform. Present data architecture decisions, platform health metrics, and consumption reports to senior and executive stakeholders Required Knowledge, Skills and Competencies Bachelor’s Degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Data Analytics, or a related field. A Salesforce Data Cloud Consultant certification is required. A Salesforce Data Architect or Marketing Cloud Consultant certification would be an added advantage. Minimum 5 years of experience in Data Architecture, Data Engineering, or the Salesforce Ecosystem. 3+ years of hands-on experience specifically within Salesforce Data Cloud. Proven track record in a high-volume Retail or Fintech environment managing millions of records. Expert SQL Proficiency for complex Data Cloud Calculated Insights (PostgreSQL Amazon Redshift syntax). Expert understanding of Data Modeling, DMO mapping, and relational data models. Comprehensive knowledge of the MarTech Ecosystem, including Data Cloud interaction with Marketing Cloud and Personalization (Interaction Studio). Strategic understanding of Identity Resolution (fuzzy vs. exact matching logic). Literacy in consumption-based software models and budget optimization. Practical application of POPIA and GDPR compliance within data architecture. Experience with REST API and Streaming Ingestion into Data Cloud Knowledge of Marketing Cloud Connect configuration and Connected Audiences setup Familiarity with event-driven architecture or real-time streaming technologies (e.g., Kafka, GCP Pub Sub, Dataflow, Apigee) for POS Fintech source system integration Understanding of GCP and Salesforce Data Cloud integration patterns, including BigQuery zero-copy connector, GCP-based data pipelines feeding Data Cloud, and the strategic value of the GCP + Salesforce "Better Together" approach to hyper-personalisation We are looking for a candidate with a strong blend of technical expertise and commercial acumen in a high-volume retail or fintech environment. Competencies: Devotion to quality, Structuring, Performing under pressure, Initiative, Leadership, Organisational sensitivity, Innovating, Analysing and forming opinions Closing date: Friday, 12 June 2026 If you are interested and meet the minimum requirements, please submit your CV. If you do not hear from us within 4 weeks of the closing date of this position, please regard your application as being unsuccessful. PEP strives for equal opportunity in terms of its employment equity guidelines.

Title: Business Systems Analyst (Cape Town)
Description: Business Systems Analyst We are seeking an experienced Business Systems Analyst to join a delivery-focused team within a leading financial services client. This is a 12-month contract role, open to Cape Town or Johannesburg-based professionals, working within a collaborative, delivery-driven environment. You will work closely with cross-functional teams across business and technology, supporting digital initiatives within the Santam environment. This role is ideal for a hands-on, systems-orientated analyst who thrives in fast-paced delivery settings and is passionate about translating business needs into effective system solutions. Key responsibilities and output areas include the following: Business & Systems Analysis Elicit, analyse, and document business, functional, and system requirements across digital initiatives. Translate business needs into system specifications, including user stories, integration requirements, and acceptance criteria. Support system-related delivery across digital platforms, integrations, and technology-enabled change. Work closely with developers, architects, and product teams to ensure requirements are clearly understood and implemented. Stakeholder Engagement & Collaboration Facilitate workshops, interviews, and working sessions with business and technical stakeholders. Act as the bridge between business users and technical teams, ensuring alignment and clarity across delivery. Communicate complex system concepts in a clear, practical way to support decision-making and delivery progress. Analysis in Practice Conduct current-state and future-state system and process analysis to identify gaps, risks, and improvement opportunities. Analyse system flows, integrations, and data to support solution design and prioritisation. Support backlog refinement, sprint planning, and prioritisation alongside product owners and delivery teams. Consulting Mindset in Action Build trusted relationships quickly within a client environment. Operate confidently in ambiguity, bringing structure, clarity, and momentum. Balance detailed systems analysis with delivery pace in a digital transformation context. Key Requirements 5+ years’ experience as a Business Analyst or Business Systems Analyst within complex organisations. Proven experience within financial services; exposure to insurance environments is highly beneficial. Strong experience in systems analysis, including integrations, APIs, or digital platforms. Hands-on experience with requirements elicitation, process modelling, and systems documentation. Experience working in delivery-led, Agile or hybrid environments. Relevant degree or equivalent experience in Business Analysis, Information Systems, IT, or related fields. Certifications (e.g. IIBA, Agile BA, or equivalent) are advantageous. Technical & Domain Knowledge Strong understanding of business and systems analysis practices and techniques. Experience working across Agile, hybrid, or waterfall delivery models. Exposure to digital transformation, system implementation, or platform modernisation initiatives. Understanding of system integrations, data flows, and technical solution environments. Please Note: As all iqbusiness roles require honesty in the handling of or access to cash, finances, financial systems, or confidential information, our recruitment process requires the following background checks: credit, criminal, ID, and qualification verification. iqbusiness is committed to sustainable growth and transformation. We embrace diversity and employ previously disadvantaged individuals.

Title: Technical Architect
Description: Who We Are VML, part of WPP, is a leading creative company that combines brand experience, customer experience, and commerce, creating connected brands to drive growth. VML is celebrated for its innovative and human first, award-winning work for blue chip client partners including AstraZeneca, Colgate-Palmolive, Dell, Ford, Microsoft, Nestlé, The Coca-Cola Company, and Wendy's. The agency is recognized by the Forrester Wave™ Reports, as a Leader among Marketing Creative and Content Service Providers, Commerce Services, Global Digital Experience Services, Global Marketing Services and, most recently, Marketing Measurement & Optimization. In addition, VML's specialist health network, VML Health, is one of the world's largest and most awarded health agencies. VML's global network is powered by 26,000 talented people across 55+ markets, with principal offices in Kansas City, New York, Detroit, London, São Paulo, Shanghai, Singapore, and Sydney. About WPP WPP is the trusted growth partner for the world's leading brands. We unite cutting-edge media intelligence and data solutions, world-class creativity, next-generation production, transformative enterprise solutions and expert strategic counsel in a single company – powered by exceptional talent and our agentic marketing platform, WPP Open, to help our clients navigate change, capture opportunity and deliver transformational growth. For more information, visit WPP.com. Overview As a Senior Technical Solutions Architect within the VML EMEA Enablement Team, you will be a pivotal figure in shaping the technical direction and delivery of strategic solutions for our diverse client portfolio. You will bridge the gap between client vision, business requirements, and technical execution, leading the entire solutions lifecycle from initial engagement and scoping through to design, development support, and successful deployment. This role demands a strong blend of technical expertise, client-facing acumen, and leadership, driving innovation and fostering a culture of excellence within our development teams. Key responsibilities Client Engagement & Strategy: Engage with existing and prospective clients to deeply understand their business challenges and strategic objectives. Lead technical client engagements, from initial scoping and requirements gathering to crafting comprehensive solution proposals and detailed solution design documentation. Collaborate closely with Client Leads and Business Development teams to gain a holistic understanding of client expectations, business constraints, and opportunities. Solution Design & Delivery Oversight: Define project scope, technical specifications, and architectural frameworks for software solutions of medium to high complexity. Present solutions directly to clients, providing clear feedback, ensuring solution alignment with business goals, and communicating project updates. Prepare and deliver reports, presentations, and demonstrations to keep clients informed on project progress, integration points, deployment strategies, and any technical considerations or challenges. Technical Leadership & Team Support: Act as a primary technical liaison between clients and development teams, translating business needs into technical requirements and vice versa. Provide expert technical solution recommendations and support the operational delivery of solutions across various platforms. Drive process excellence and technical innovation, particularly in the seamless handover of technical solutions and documentation to development teams. Actively engage with developers, consultants, and project managers to ensure successful execution of engagements. Technical Specialisation & Project Involvement: Contribute to and lead efforts in: + Solution design and architecture (medium to high complexity). + Defining and adhering to data and technology governance principles. + Advanced scripting and query development. + Developing robust data mapping and extract strategies. + Designing and optimising relational data models and data schemas. + Providing high-level technical support for complex client requests. + Participating in and leading advanced technical projects. + Designing and implementing integrations between marketing and data platforms. + Developing web services and API solutions. + Leading custom reporting and custom application development projects. + HTML creation for webpages and emails. + Webpage builds using the jQuery framework. Leverage native scripting languages to solve requirements in the most effective, optimal, and scalable manner. Mentorship & Continuous Improvement: Actively mentor and facilitate knowledge transfer within the broader developer team, fostering growth and technical excellence. Stay abreast of new technologies, industry trends, and emerging best practices, assessing their applicability and championing their introduction to the development team and wider organisation where beneficial. Required Skills & Experience Extensive Marketing Technology Expertise: Extensive hands-on experience with marketing technology stacks covering marketing data, production, content management, planning, implementation, and various channel use (e.g., email, web, mobile, CRM, Audience Management, and Analytics). Proficiency with popular enterprise technology stacks including, but not limited to, Google (e.g., GCP, GA4), Adobe (e.g., Experience Cloud, AEM), Salesforce Marketing Cloud, Salesforce Data Cloud, Salesforce Personalisation, AWS, and Snowflake. Core Marketing Functional Experience: Demonstrated expertise in: Customer Data Platforms (CDP) Personalisation strategies and implementation Direct Digital marketing channels (Mobile, Email, SMS, MMS & Push) CMS & Content arbitration automation Web Analytics and data interpretation Strategic & Client-Facing Acumen: Exceptional ability to understand unique business requirements of customers across multiple industries and translate these needs into a compelling technical vision and roadmap, effectively presenting solutions to both technical and non-technical stakeholders. Solution Engineering Leadership: A proven track record in leading the design and delivery of complex technical solutions, from concept to deployment. Middleware & Integration Architecture: Ability to architect and develop robust middleware solutions for integrating various technology stacks and platforms, including API design and development. Data Expertise: Strong experience with SQL, relational databases (e.g., MSSQL, MySQL), advanced scripting for data manipulation, and comprehensive data modelling. Team Leadership & Mentorship: Experience in leading and managing technical or development teams. Process & Methodologies: Experience working effectively within a Project Management Office (PMO) framework and applying best practice development methodologies (e.g., Agile, Scrum). Communication & Collaboration: Solid oral and written interpersonal communication skills, strong relationship-building abilities, and proven capability to work effectively as part of a cross-functional team in varied, fast-paced environments. Time Management: Proven ability to manage multiple priorities, projects, and deadlines effectively in a dynamic, high-pressure environment. Continuous Learning & Passion: An inquisitive, practical, and passionate approach to technology, with a commitment to continuous learning, sharing knowledge, and staying abreast of industry trends. Travel: Willingness and ability to travel occasionally for client engagements or team meetings within the EMEA region. Experience Threshold: Some experience in hands-on marketing technology or solutions architecture experience. Preferred Skills & Experience (Beneficial) Candidates demonstrating some or all the following will be at an advantage: Academic Background: Bachelor's degree in information systems or computer science, or a closely related technical field. Advanced CDP & CRM Knowledge: Deeper experience with CDP & CRM platforms, including an understanding of complex data models, database marketing principles, and customer product lifecycle management strategies. Certifications: Relevant Data or Architecture certifications (e.g., AWS Certified Solutions Architect, Salesforce Certified Technical Architect, Google Cloud Architect, Azure Solutions Architect). Development Experience: Knowledge or hands-on experience with software development in mobile (iOS or Android) or web development (e.g., PHP, ASP.NET Core, Python). Industry Experience: Previous experience working within the Automotive industry. We believe the best work happens when we're together, fostering creativity, collaboration, and connection. That's why we've adopted a hybrid approach, with teams in the office an average of four days a week. If you require accommodations or flexibility, please discuss this with the hiring team during the interview process. WPP (VML) is an equal opportunity employer and considers applicants for all positions without discrimination or regard to characteristics. We are committed to fostering a culture of respect in which everyone feels they belong and has the same opportunities to progress in their careers. VML is a WPP agency (NYSE: WPP). Title: Systems Analyst: JG11

We offer a comprehensive range of investment solutions, designed to assist in the creation and preservation of wealth. Our offering encompasses local investments, including fixed term investments and investments with guarantees, international investments, retirement saving solutions, and retirement income solutions. While each solution has its own distinct purpose, they all share the world-class quality and commitment that have come to distinguish Glacier. What will you do? The Systems Analyst will play a critical role as a problem solver and be the person who assists in defining and enabling business change in an agile environment. Your primary responsibility is to design and deliver solutions that help optimize processes and reduce inefficiencies. This role will be based at the Glacier Head Office in Cape Town. Facilitation of work-sessions, database design and solution workshops to gather information, elicit and finalize functional requirements as well as transfer knowledge. Analyze how the business requirements will be met by introducing changes to the application s or creating modern technology implementations. Examine existing systems to identify areas for improvement and integration as part of the solution design, consider non-functional requirements that describe the characteristics of the system e.g., security, performance, maintainability, scalability, usability, and reliability of a product. Ensure system designs are aligned with business goals & requirements and ensuring that requirements are accurate and complete. Produce fit-for-purpose specifications documenting the solution. Produce data models, activity, component flow, functional decomposition and data flow diagrams using Enterprise Architect Visio Confluence. Document and implement, and guidelines to ensure that best solutions and designs are implemented and consistent with group architecture principals. Ensure that all stakeholders are engaged and kept up to date. Post implementation support to business and IT. What will make you successful in this role? Qualifications and experience Matric Grade 12 Bachelor’s degree in computer science, Information Systems, or a related field. Minimum 5 years’ Software development experience in the Financial Services industry Experience in Business Analysis or similar role which shows Analysis capability Technical Experience Experience in design and support workflow solutions Strong relational database expertise 5+ years (SQL) Experience in DB2 and SAP Hana beneficial Experience with APIs 5+ years Knowledge of AWS and Snowflake Exposure to C , XML, JSON, SOA Experience analyzing operational needs to design, automate, publish, test, monitor and report on business workflows in AzureDevOps Experience in SQL stored procedures essential Demonstrated knowledge of object-oriented analysis and design Strong data analysis skills Strong Application Design expertise Exposure to SCRUM essential and other Agile methodologies. Exposure to Domain Driven Design Competencies Problem-solving and analysis skills Ability to perform well under pressure Planning and organizing Decision-making ability Attention to detail Attributes Honesty, integrity, and respect Positive, enthusiastic attitude Professional work standards Ability to motivate self and others Qualification and Experience Degree or Diploma and the required Certification with 10 to 12 years related experience. Knowledge and Skills Component level design based on Technology Architecture Model System Analysis System and Database design System testing and reviews Security and performance assessments Personal Attributes Balances stakeholders - Contributing through others Financial acumen - Contributing through others Plans and aligns - Contributing through others Communicates effectively - Contributing through others Build a successful career with us We’re all about building strong, lasting relationships with our employees. We know that you have hopes for your future – your career, your personal development and of achieving great things. We pride ourselves in helping our employees to realise their worth. Through its five business clusters – Sanlam Fintech, Sanlam Life and Savings, Sanlam Investment Group, Sanlam Allianz, Santam, as well as MiWay and the Group Office – the group provides many opportunities for growth and development.
